Chk-in # Order Exam 4721223 ooo1 26030 MM MAMMO DIAG BILAT DIGITAL Ord Diag: LT BREAST NODULE/PAIN AXILLA

REPORT: DIAGNOSTIC DIGITAL MAMMOGRAM

REASON FOR EXAM: 25-YEAR-OLD FEMALE WITH LEFT BREAST NODULE

Standard craniocaudal and oblique views of the breasts were obtained. This is patient's baseline study.

I do not see a mammographic correlate for the palpable abnormality in the upper-outer left breast. No spiculated masses or suspicious microcalcifications are seen.

recommend clinical correlation for the palpable abnormality.

IMPRESSION: Benign findings in the breast. BI-RADS 2. Based on the Mammographic findings routine follow-up would be appropriate.

Today's study was evaluated in conjunction with computer aided detection.

PLEASE NOTE THE FOLLOWING ACR RECOMMENDATIONS: A: Only 80% of breast cancer can be diagnosed by

mammography .

B: Negative mammogram does not exclude cancer in clinically palpable abnormalities. Biopsy should be done based on ultrasound findings and clinical judgment.

FINAL CONTINUED Page i

CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS

1, CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, AND MEDICAL TREATMENT: ;

| voluntarily consent to care Involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating physicians, including his/her assistants or designees. | consent to Allied Health students observing and participating in my care under the supervision of a qualified professional. This consent also includes testing for communicable and blood-borne infectlous diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunedeficiency virus (HIV), if a physician orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. No guarantee has been given to me as to the results that may be obtained from my care.

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE INDEPENDENT PRACTITIONERS:

In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is provided by physicians who are independent practitioners and not employees or agents of the Hospital. Independent practitioners may include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency room physicians, pathologists, surgeons and surgical assistants. There are also other health care providers in the Hospital who are independent practitioners and not employees or agents of the Hospital including, but not limited to certified registered — nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners and psychologists. You will receive a separate bill for these services or the Hospitat bill you receive will have separate charges for services of physicians and other independent practitioners. Charges for these services are established by

the physicians and other independent practitioners.

3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:

1 authorize the Hospital and the independent practitioners on its medical staff to use and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures inciude, but are not limited to, providing informatian fo: (a) any person, company, ar entity (guch as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that ts or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital; (6) any independent practitioner or ambulance company providing services for me; {c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, and physicians; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; and (e) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.

4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:

| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. If | have requested a private room, ! agree to pay the additional cost for a private room. Payment of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to ather arrangements.

agree to the assignment of ail third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any physician and independent practitioner providing services for me. | agree to pay the Hospital, physicians, and independent practitioners for all charges for services which are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited ta a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medicaily necessary. After reasonable notice, any unpaid account may be turned aver to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in collecting my account.

5. HOSPITAL IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR LOSS OF PERSONAL ARTICLES:

| understand that the Hospital maintains a safe far the safekeeping of money and valuabies and that the Hospital is not liable for the foss of or damage to money, jewelry, glasses, dentures, documents, clothing, or other items of personal property untess deposited in the hospital safe for safekeeping.

6. SMOKE-FREE ENVIRONMENT: This Hospital maintains a smoke-free environment. Smoking is prohibited by health care personnel, patients, and visitors except in designated areas outside the building.

7. DISCHARGE:

| agree to prompt discharge fallowing the order of my physician that medical treatment is completed and that continued hospitalization is unnecessary. Late discharges will be subject to additional room charges.

DICTATING: Travis Gilbert, DO DATE OF SURGERY: [DOB-6539]

DATE OF BIRTH: [DOB-6556]

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSES: High-grade squamous intraepithelial Lesion.

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion. PROCEDURE PERFORMED: Loop electrosurgical excision procedure (LEEP). SURGEON: Dr. Travis Gilbert.

ANESTHESIA: Gensral LMA.

COMPLICATIONS: None.

SPECIMENS: Transition zone of the cervix.

INDICATIONS: This 27-year-old female was found to have an high-grade squamous intraepithelial Lesion with moderate dysplasia by colposcopy and cervical biopsy in the office. She was counseled on LEEP procedure because of this. She agreed. We counseled her on the risks of the proceedure, bleeding, infection, injury to adjacent organs, as well as risk 9£ preterm labor and delivery later on in life.

DESCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE: The patient was taken to the operating room, given general LMA anesthesia, and prepped and draped in the dorsal lithotomy position using candy~cane stirrups. The patient was sterilely prepared. The cervix was ciraumferentially injected with 1% lidocaine with epinephrine. We then put some acetic on, delineating the transition zone. With one pass, we excised the transition zone of the carvix using a loop. We then used the bali cautery to Gauterize the entire bed. We placed astringent and the procedure was finished. We removed the speculum and took the patient to the recovery in stable condition.

DEPARTMENT OF ANESTHESIOLOGY ~— 728945044 ADULT Phase Il, S.D.S. Post-op ORDERS

73201 4

ALLERGIES: HEIGHT: WEIGHT:

1. Vital Signs: VS every 30 minutes

2. Fluids: Discontinue IV when the patient is taking adequate oral fluids

3, Pain Management:

Morphine 0.05mg/kg (dose = mg) one time. Notify the Anesthesiologist for inadequate analgesia

4, Nausea/Vomiting: 0 Ondansetron 4mg slow [VP PRN times one dose for nausea/vomiting

( Dexamethasone 4mg slow IVP PRN times one dose for nausea/vomiting D Metoclopramide 10mg slow IVP PRN times one dose for nausea/vomiting Notify the Anesthesiologist for inadequate relief

5. Throat Discomfort: Cepacol Throat Lozenges one every 2 hours PRN

6. Discharge:

Discharge patient to the care of a responsible adult when discharge criteria are met. (Post-Op evaluation score of 10 or greater).

DEPARTMENT OF ANESTHESIOLOGY — ADULT PACU ORDERS

ALLERGIES:

HEIGHT: WEIGHT:

1, Vital Signs: VS every 5 minutes for 15 minutes and every 15 minutes thereafter. 2. Oxygenation: A. Monitor oxygen saturation while in P.A.C.U. Oxygen therapy on each patient at 8L face mask or 4L nasal cannula. Discontinue oxygen if O2 Sat greater than 92% on room air. B. O23L via nasal cannula overnight if patient unable to maintain O2 Sat greater than 92%. C. Discontinue in AM if ©O2 Sat greater than 92% 3. I.V. Therapy: A. EV. fluid orders per surgeon unless specified by Anesthesiologist B. Call Anesthesiologist for additional LV. fluids bolus for Hypotension 4. Pain Management: Choose one of the following: . O Fentanyl 0.5 meg/kg (dose = ) 1.V. every 5 minutes for a pain scale of 2-5. Fentanyl 1.0 mcg/kg (dose = ) LV. every 5 minutes for a pain scale of 5-10. Do not exceed 2mcg/kg/hour.

( Morphine 2mg IV. every 10 minutes for a pain scale of 2-5. Morphine 4mg LV. every 5 minutes for a pain scale of 5-10. If PCA to be used Post-Op, may titrate TV pushes every 5 minutes giving twice the one hour limit as written by the surgeon. Do not exceed 20m¢/hour.

O Hydromorphone 0.25mg 1.V. every 10 minutes for a pain scale of 2-5 Hydromorphone 0.5mg I.V. every 10 minutes for a pain scale of 5-10 Deo Not excced Z2mg/hour. Peak effect may be delayed up to 20 minutes.

O Epidural orders, call the Anesthesiologist

5. Shivering: A. Ensure patients temperature is greater than 97, if not, place convective heating blanket. B. Meperidine 12.5 mg LV. may repeat once in 5 minutes 6. Nausea/Vomiting: Choose one of the following: O Ondansetron 4mg slow IVP PRN x1 for nausea/vomiting O Dexamethasone 4mg slow [VP PRN x1 for nausea/vomiting O Metoclopramide 10mg slow IVP PRN x1 for nausea/vomiting O Promethazine 12.5 mg slow IVP PRN x 1 for nausea/vomiting. May repeat in 30 minutes x 1 dose.

MITA

728945044

Continued:

7. Additional Orders: CALL BEFORE IMPLEMENTING!

D Atropine 0.4mg IV for heart rate less than 40

CO Albuterol nebs 2.5mg/0.5ml in 2.5ml NS, PRN wheezing

0 Labetolol 5mg slow IVP repeat every 10 minutes PRN SBP greater than 180, or DBP greater than 100 Do Not give if heart rate is less than 60

O Hydralazine S5mg slow IVP repeat every 10 minutes PRN SBP greater than 180, or DBP greater than 100

CG Lidocaine 100mg IVP for PVC’s greater than 6 per minute

O Midazolam 11mg IVP may repeat once in 10 min PRN anxiety

O Diphenhydramine 25mg IV may repeat once in 5 min PRN itching

4) Ketorolac 30mg IV for pain and discomfort consult with anesthesiologist and surgeon before administering.

O Naloxone 0.04mg (0.1 ml) IV titrate 0.04 mg IV every one minute PRN respiratory depression until patient arouses and respirations are greater than 12/minute

OD Accucheck for diabetics who are presently on insulin or if requested by anesthesia provider before leaving PACU; If results greater than 150 inform the Anesthesiologist for insulin coverage

O Convective warming blanket for temp less than 35C.

8. May contact the Anesthesiologist for additional orders. 9. Discharge:

Discharge patient from PACU when discharge criteria is met. (Aldrete/PAR score = 8 or above). 10, Other:

728945044 7414/2011 BAPTIST REGIONAL MEDICAL CENTER [DOB-6556] 27 ¥ 728945 PHARMACY AND THERAPEUTICS COMMITTEE

728045044 ADULT PRE-OP ORDERS

ALLERGIES: Cox eOOnuLY 4 WYER

HEIGHT: WEIGHT:

1. All patients to be NPO after midnight, except for patients having afternoon surgery who may have solids up to 8 hours prior to surgery and clear liquids up to 4 hours prior to surgery. 2. Instruct all patients to take their normal dose of routine PO meds with a small sip of water at home, or in $.D.S. (exceptions are Insulin, Oral Antihyperglycemics, Anticoagulants, Diuretics and MAO inhibitors). 3. Start 18g fV catheter (may use lidocaine 1% skin wheal for local anesthesia) with 1000ml LR on all patients scheduled for any type of anesthesia (See exceptions). Use extension sets on all IV tubing. Exceptions: A) Patients who have Chronic Renal Failure, start 1000m! nermal saline at KVO rate. B) Patients scheduled for Cataract Extraction place a PID except diabetic patients (follow diabetic protocol). C) Patients who are undergoing a T.U.R.P. are to have 1000m] NS 4. Diabetic Protocol: A) Blood glucose meter reading with placement of [V, or AM of surgery if IV already in place. Call Anesthesiologist if less than 80 or greater than 200 before proceeding further, B) For insulin dependent TV DSNS at a rate of 7ml/kg over two hours bolus followed by 2ml/kg/hr rate. (Bolus = ml/hr x 2 hours, then rate = mi/hr.)} C) Once IV fluid initiated, give % of the patient’s normal AM insulin (dose = ). 5. Pre-Testing: B-HCG test on all females of child bearing age (typically 12-60) unless surgically sterilized. QO Serum K on patients taking diuretics, or if clinically indicated QO Hbg/Hct when clinically indicated QO EKG tracing with interpretation if clinically indicated. Indication: GQ Chest X-Ray ONLY when clinically indicated QO PT/PTT, INR if clinically indicated 6. Pre-Op Meds: J4 Midazolam (Versed) 2mg IVP may repeat times PRN for sedation Metoclopramide (Reglan) |Omg IVP Famotidine (Pepcid) 20mg I'vP Bicitra 30m] PO on call to OR Dexamethasone mg IVP Scopalamine 1.5mg transdermal patch to mastoid process Institute AHA guidelines for Sub-Acute Bacterial Endocarditis Prophylaxis, if indicated by history and type of surgery (e.g. G.I., G.U., and Oral Sx) Inform surgeon prior to administration Albuterol 2.5mg/0.5ml in 2.5m] NS by nebulizer If IV dye allergy prophylaxis/beded: Famotidine 20 mg IVP x 1 dog Diphenhydramine 50 mg IWVP/# 1 dose Hydrocortisone sodium gu¥ Physician Signature:

iis Smoking/Tobacco Cessation Counseling

Why Should You Stop Smoking or Using Tobacco? Smoking/Tobacco use causes CANCER to grow. Smoking/Tobacco use causes HEART and LUNG DISEASE. Smoking/Tobacco use is EXPENSIVE. Smoking/Tobacco use quickens the AGING process. Second-hand smoke HURTS your family and friends. Smoking damages TASTEBUDS and discolors your TEETH and NAILS. Smoking increases NERVOUSNESS.

What are the Benefits of Quitting? Your chances of getting cancer decrease. Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours. YOU SAVE MONEY! Your taste and smell will improve. You will have more energy. You will live longer! How to Quit: Ready — Set a quit date and stick to it —not a single puff more. Set — Get support from family, friends, co-workers, doctor, or other health care providers — get group, individual, or telephone counseling. Go — Learn new ways to live healthy: « Change your routine to distract yourself from smoking. « Reduce stress. « Eat a healthy diet and stay active. = Drink plenty of water and other fluids. Medication That Can Help: Talk to your healthcare provider about whether medication is right for you. Common medications available to help you quit: « ZYBAN — www. zyban.com— available by prescription * Nicotine gum —www.nicorettegum. com -— available over-the-counter *® Nicotine inhaler ~— available by prescription » Nicotine nasal spray — www, nicobrevin. com — available by prescription * Nicotine patch — www.nicodermCQ. com — available over-the-counter

For More Information:

* American Heart Association [PHONE-6489]

= American Lung Association [PHONE-69] or 1-800-LUNG-USA = Cooper — Clayton Smoking Cessation Program [PHONE-70]

Sponsored by BRMC/Laurel County Health Department lf you are a non-smoker, please feel free to share this information with a family member or friend.

This information Is About Your Follow Up Care

An appointment has been made for you on JULY 27, 2011 @ 9:15 AM to see Travis Giibert,MD. You can reach Or. Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-6484], 1 Trillium Way Suite 200, Corbin, KY, 40701.

This Information ts About Procedures

LEEP (logp electrosurgicat excision procedure)

You have had a procedure called “LEEP” done today. "LEEP” stands for “loop electrosurgical excision procedure”. This is a procedure used to remove abnormal tissue from the cervix afier an abnormal Pap smear, LEEP is done using a thin wire (gop thathas electricat current running through It. The wire laop “shaves off" the cervical tissue thathas abnormal cells. Italso seals off the blood vessels tn the tissue to prevent bleeding atter the procedure.

When you go home, follow these instructions:

* You may return to your nermal activities today.

* Wear a sanitary pad for possible vaginal discharge or mild bleeding. This may last a few days, or a few weeks.

Avoid using tampons or douches for up to 4 weeks. Your doctor will tell you when it is okay to start using these products again.

Avoid sexual Intercourse for up to 4 weeks. Your dactor will tell you speciically when you can return to having sex,

Try not to become pregnant for at least 3 months.

ACETAMINOPHEN and CODEINE

Take this medicine with food In the following dose; Tylenol #3: 1-2 tablets by mouth evary 4 hours if needed for pain.

This [s a mixture of two paln relievers. Together they can relieve moderate to severe pain. Acotaminophen/codeine may also be prescribed to reduce a cough. This combination medicine may be used for other reasons, as prescribed by your doctor.

Side effects may include:

* sleepiness or dizziness

* upset stomach

* constipation (hard stools)

Other side effects may occur, but ara notas common. Allergy would show up as: rash or itching, facial or throat swelling, wheezing or shortness of breath. This medicine can be habit forming if used for a long period of time,

Follow these Instructions: * Never take more of this medicine than prescribed. * Read the labels on non-prescription cough and cold medicines. Many contain acetaminophen. To avoid an overdose, do not take any other medicines thal contain acetaminophen. Deo not share this medicine with others as this medicine is a controlled-substance. Sharing this medicine with others is against the law. Watch tor signs of dependence:

» feeling that you “cannot live without this medicine".

* younged more of this medicine than before to gat the same rallef. To avoid constipation while taking this medicine:

* Drink plenty of liquids. Try to drink 8 to 10 elght-ounce glasses of water or Juice each day.

* Include extra fibar in your dist.

* Exercise dalty. Ro not drink alcohol, drive or operate machinary until you know how this medicine affects you. Store this medicine away from heat, moisture or direct light. It you are taking this on a regular schedule and you miss a@ dosa, take it as soon as possible, Ifitis almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose and return to your regular schedule. Do not doubls the doses.

Tatk with your doctor before taking any other medicines {including herbals) as you may require addittonal monitoring espectally:

LOGICARE

CIGARETTE SMOKING.

This is your greatest health risk! This is a risk that you can control, The facts ars clear that cigarette smoking will shorten your life. Smoking can cause many iiinesses along the way. If you need help to quit smoking, talk to your regular doctor. There are many medications and ways to help you quilt, you and your. doctor can decide together which is best for you.

Here are the some of the benefits of quitting smoking: , Your body begins to heal from the effects of smoking within 12 hours of your last cigarette.

Your heart and lungs start repairing the damage caused by cigarette smoke,

You will have a better sense of taste and smell.

You will have more energy and look and fee! healthier.

You will breathe easier and your smoker's cough will go away.

You will lower your risk for illness and death from heart disease, stroke, chronic bronchitis, emphysema, lung cancer, and other types of cancer.

You will contribute to cteaner air, especially for children at risk for illness because they breathe other's cigarette smoke.

You may save hundreds af dollars every year.

Here are some tips to get you started:

if you are not on fluid restriction due to congestive heart failure or kidney disease or other @ness, drink at least eight glasses of walera day, This wil help to flush out the toxins in your system.

When you feel the urge to smoke —

Deep breathing exercises. Try to keep calm and cool. Take slow deep breaths, in though your nose and oul through your mouth.

Do something else: take a walk around the block. Try to stay away from activities which you would normally hava done while smoking a cigaretta.

Oiscuss with a friend or family member. Talking with a support person can help you feel better.

Delay your urge. The urge to smoke will pass in three to five minutes. Count ta 100 or 200. Think pleasant thoughts.

You may be billed separately for the services of these practitioners or the bill you receive may include separate charges for their services. These charges are established by the practitioners.

This section is to be completed by the licensed caregiver performing procedure.

RISKS, BENEFITS, & ALTERNATIVES. The physician has explained to me: (1) the nature of the proposed procedure; (2} the potential risks, benefits and side effects, including potential problems that might occur during recuperation; (3) the likelihood of achieving goals; (4) reasonahle alternatives to the procedure, if any; (5) the relevant benefits and risks, and side effects associated with those alternatiyé cluding the possible results of not receiving care, treatment, and services, likelihood of achieving goals and (6), pySxible f not receiving caré, treatment and services

Physicians signa bore Date

BLOOD TRANSFUSION AND BLOOD PRODUCTS RISKS, BENEFITS, & ALTERNATIVES- If you wish not to receive blood or blood products please request to sign a refusal form. 1) Some surgical procedures may require blood transfusions or blood products. My doctor has informed me that [ may need to have blood products administered during the course of my treatment. 2) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products involves the introduction of the product into one of my veins, commonly in the arm using a needle. I understand that the amount of blood products administered is determined by my doctor based upon my particular needs. 3) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products may expose me to certain risks, Minor or temporary reactions, such as slight bruising, swelling, or Jocal infection of the area where the needle pierces the skin is not uncommon. Non-serious reactions may include headache, fever, or a mild skin reaction, such as itching or a rash. Rare, but serious reactions are possible such as the transmission of diseases including hepatitis and AIDS, which require lengthy treatment and can be fatal. It has been further explained that while procedures for screening blood products for diseases such as AIDS and hépatitis are effective, these procedures are not perfect and there is a risk of exposure to AIDS, hepatitis, or other blood-borne disease. I have taken all of these risks into consideration and have decided that the benefits of receiving blood products outweigh the risks. 4) I have been made aware of the reasonably available alternatives to the administration of blood products and the risks and benefits of those alternatives including autologous blood transfusion, directed donation and not having any blood products administered. I understand that [ may be able to donate one of several units of my own blood to be used during planned elective surgery and that my doctor must determine my ability to donate for myself. This is called autologous transfusion. If I choose to have an autologous transfusion, I understand that the last unit of blood must be donated one week prior to expected use. I also understand that I may choose a directed donation. A directed donation means I am allowed to select my own donors. There is no scientific evidence that directed donations are safer but | understand many patients feel more comfortable designating their own donors. The designated donor can donate within thirty (30) days of the anticipated need for the blood. It takes at least seventy-two (72) hours for a unit from a directed donor to be processed and available for transfusion. These arrangements must be made by my doctor.

SURGERY/PROCEDURE INFORMED CONSENT

SALES REPRESENTATIVES-I understand that there may be a representative from a company that fumishes equipment to surgeons and hospitals present in the room during my surgical procedure. I also understand that his/her function is strictly to observe and advise, and that this person has no part in my direct care.

ANESTHESIA-I understand that this procedure may require anesthesia. An anesthesiologist has or will explain the anesthesia alternatives. Complications from any type of anesthetic are infrequent, but do occur. Complications are more frequent for patients in poor health than for patients in good health. Complications associated with all types of anesthesia include temporary or permanent damage to teeth, lips, nose, throat, voice box, windpipe, eyes, nerves, or blood vessels. Drug reaction may occur and may be minor or serious even requiring resuscitation, Breathing problems may develop.

Headache, back pain, some degree of awareness, infection, nausea and/or vomiting may occur. There are some very serious complications, which are uncommon, but can occur. These include paralysis, brain damage, or death. There are other potential complications depending on your situation; however, it is not possible to advise you of every imaginable complication. The purpose of this is not to frighten you, but to properly inform you. Serious complications are unlikely. The benefits of surgery and anesthesia, except in extreme circumstances, outweigh the potential risks, I agree that the potential benefit of the procedure(s) outweighs the potential risks of anesthesia.

I understand that my procedure is scheduled to be done with one of the following:

General Anesthesia-monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist with or without a CRNA

Monitored Anesthesia Care-possible sedation, monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist or a CRNA Regionat Anesthesia-monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist or a CRNA

Local Anesthesia-care managed by the Physician and monitored by a registered nurse

Moderate Sedation-care managed by the Physician and monitored by a registered nurse

I understand that I must be:

1.) Accompanied by a responsible adult.

2.) Not take public transportation without the presence of another adult.

3.) Not drive a car, ingest alcoholic beverages, or depressant medication without direct permission from the physician, for a minimum of 24 hours.

4.) Not make any important decisions or sign any important documents or checks for 24 ta 36 hours

There is the possibility that any of the services provided above may convert to a general anesthetic. These same complications may occur with local anesthesia. It may become necessary for the anesthesiologist to render care in a life-threatening emergency situation without my previous consent. If during a local anesthetic an emergent situation arises I understand that my physician may consult with an anesthesiologist to render the necessary emergency medical care.

DNR STATUS WILL BE SUSPENDED DURING SURGERY/ PROCEDURE BUT WILL RESUME AFTER MY PROCEDURE AND RECOVERY.

Please sign that you understand this document.

Based on my physician’s explanation of the benefits and risks of this procedure and the alternatives available, I agree that the potential bencfits of the procedure(s) outweigh the potential risks involved. My physician has explained to me that all surgical procedures involve some risk, including risks such as severe loss of blood, infection, cardiac arrest and, in rare instances, death. My physician has explained to me that a first assistant or one of the physicians in his/her group may be involved in this procedure. I acknowledge that medicine is not an exact science and that no guarantees have been made to me concerning the results of the procedure(s) described above. I understand that if complications occur, additional medical treatment may be required and emergency medical treatment is available.

2, PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE INDEPENDENT PRACTITIONERS:

Ih most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is provided by physicians whe are independent practitioners and not employees or agents of the Hospital. tndependent practitioners may include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiotogists, emergency room physicians, pathologists, surgeons and surgical assistants. There are also other heatth care providers in the Hospital who are not employees or agents of the Hospital including, but not limited to certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, and psychologists. You will receive a separate hill for these services or the Hospital bill you receive will have separate charges for services of physicians and other tndependent practitioners. Charges for these services are established by the physicians and other independent practitioners.

3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:

| authorize the Hospital and the independent practitioners on its medical staff to use and disciose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disctosures include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital; (b) any independent practitioner or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, and physicians; (d) any licensing of accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain ticensure or accreditation; and (e} any other persons or entities described in the Hospital's Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. t also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.

4. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:

| agree that I am respansible for payment of my Hospital bill. if | have requested a private room, | agree to pay the additional cost for a private room, Payment of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other arrangements.

Time Seen: 12:47. Arvrived- By private vehicle. Historian- spouse and patient.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Chief Complaint: PELVIC PAIN and VAGINAL BLEEDING. This started 2 months ago, worse over the last 3 days and still present. It was abrupt in onset and has been constant,

The symptoms are described as severe. Modifying factors. Not worsened by anything. Not relieved by anything. Ths patient has had severe, constant, sharp suprapubic pelvic pain with vaginal bleeding. She has had sevare, Gonstant, sharp right-sided and left-sided lower back pain. She has had abnormal bleeding described as heavier than normal period and passing clots. No abdeminal pain, vaginal pain, flank pain, vaginal discharge or vaginal itching. Wo genital lesions, urinary fraquency, urgency of urination or hematuria. She has recently had irregular periods. They have been Longer than normal, consisted of heavier than normal flow and included the passage of clots. She has had mild pain with urination. It is described as "painful" and has occurred during urination.

Last normal menstrual period now. Gravida 3. Para 2. Abortions 1. Sexual history - sexually active. No contraception. Sexually active. (Pt had her IUD removed 3 days age and her symptoms have become worse since then. She has been using rolls of toilet paper to control her bleeding and has to change it every hour.).

Similar symptoms previously: Nons.

Recent medical care: The patient was seen recently at another facility in the office.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

No nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, black stools or headache. No fever, chills,

anorexia, eye discomfort or sore throat. No cough, difficulty breathing,

chest pain, skin rash or enlarged lymph nodes. No joint pain. Those patient

has had fatigue and dizziness. All systems ctherwise negative, except as scorded above.

ONE TRILLIUM WAY ‘ORBIN, KENTUCKY 40701 [PHONE-145] NAME +: MILLS, AMY ROOM NUMBER: W237 1P MEDICAL RECORD NUMBER: 728945 VISIT NUMBER: 70000129296 DICTATING: Travis Gilbert, DO PRIMARY CARE PROVIDER: DATE OF SURGERY: [DOB-6411] DATE OF BIRTH: [DOB-6556]

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Suspected ectopic pregnancy.

POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Left tubal pregnancy.

PROCEDURE PERFORMED: Laparoscopic left salpingectomy.

SPECIMENS REMOVED: Left tuba with ectopic.

BLOOD ADMINISTERED: None.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS: Minimal. She had about 200 mL of intra-abdominal blood. COMPLICATIONS: Nona.

ANESTHESTA; General,

SURGEON: Travis Gilbert, DO.

GRAFTS OR IMPLANTS: None.

DESCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE: The patisant was taken to the operating room, given general anesthesia, prepped and draped in the dorsal lithotomy position using Allen stirrups. The patient was sterilely prepared and draped. The bladder was drained. A manipulator was placed into the uterus. The surgeon was regloved and attention was made to the abdomen. I made a 5 mm incision into the umbilicus and inserted an Optiview trocar into the ahdomen under direct visualization using laparoscope. We filled the abdomen with cO2 gas up to i5 mmHg and placed an 8 mm trocar suprapubically in the midline and a 5 mm trocar to the left.

OPERATIVE FINDINGS:

1. Hemoperitonsum.

2. The left fallopian tube was swollen with an ectopic and was bleeding out “he end, The ends appeared to hava blunting.

wH&SCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE: We first used to the Enseal device to grasp under the fallopian tube, across the broad ligament sealing and cutting as we went along. We excised the entire fallopian tube. We placed it into a bag and removed it through the 8 mm port. We then suctioned all tha blood out of the abdomen. Irrigated, placed some Marcaine in tha pelvis for postoperative pain control,

Page 1 of 2

615 of 862




MILLS , AMY 70000129296 e*KEAS 616 of 862

removed the CO2 gas, removed the trocars, We closed skin incisions with 4-0 _Monocryl, and placed surgical adhesive over the incisions. There were no ‘omplications of the procedure.

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATH #: $14-2903 PATIENT: AMY MILLS SERVICE: [DOB-6405] DOB: [DOB-6556] (30) SEX: F PT. ID: 70000129296 RECEIVED [DOB-153] PHYSICIAN: TRAVIS GILBERT, M.D. 528-5527

REPORTED: [DOB-6403] LOCATION: BAPTIST HEALTH CORBIN

CLINICAL DATA: PROVISIONAL DX: LEFT PELVIC PAIN.

DIAGNOSIS: LEFT FALLOPIAN TUBE: ECTOPIC (TUBAL) PREGNANCY WITH ASSOCIATED HEMORRHAGE AND CHRONIC INFLAMMATION.

SPECIMEN SITE: LEFT FALLOPIAN TUBE

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen consists of a portion of distended, gray brown hemorrhagic fallopian tube measuring approximately 5 cm in length and ranging in diameter from 3 mm to 1.2 om. A disrupted area is present, from which gray tan papillary type tissue protrudes, measuring up to 1 cmin diameter. Serial sectioning reveals distention of the tubal lumen by hemorrhagic clot material. A detached fragment of clot material is also received in the specimen container, measuring up to 2 x 1.3.x 0.8 cm. Tan papillary tissue is focally seen at the edge of the clot material. There is also a third piece of tissue which is tan and glistening and measures 1.5 x 0.8 x 0.5 cm. Sectioning reveals a pinpoint lumen, suggestive of proximal tube. Multiple representative sections are submitted in two blocks. (CM)

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION: Microscopic examination performed.

CM/ds

CHRISTINE MEECE, M.D.

Thank you for choosing Baptist Health Corbin. The following information will help you return te your best level of health. Everyone's recovery is different.

Normal Discharge Home This information Is About Your Follow Up Care

An appointment has been made for you on [DOB-5254] at 2:45 pm to see Travis Gilbert,MD. You can reach Dr. Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-5253], 1 Trillium Way Suite 200, Corbin, KY, 40701.

This infermation Is About Procedures

ECTOPIC PREGNANCY With SALPINGECTOMY.

An ectopic pregnancy is a pregnancy that occurs outside of the uterus, The fertilized egg started growing in your fallopian tube instead of your uterus. Many times It is unknown why this occurs but most often happens when the tubes narrow. The fertilized egg cannot survive and must be surgically removed, Complete healing will take several weeks.

{includes ‘Female reproductive system showing possible ectopic pregnancy sites’ graphic)

There is an incision Into your abdomen to remove the section or all of the atfected fallopian tube. This witl end your present pregnancy. Yaur ability ta get pregnant again is slightly decreased with one tube instead of two tubes. You CAN get pregnant again with one fallopian tube.

Follow these instructions; Slowly increase your activity, Take short walks and get plenty of rest, Climb stairs slowly and use the hand rail. No pushing, pulling or straining (vacuuming, shoveling, sweeping etc.) You may take a shower in AM after your procedure.. Check with your doctor befare driving. Check with your docter before resuming sexual activity. Do not use douches, tampons or hava intercourse for 6 weeks unless otherwise instructad. * To avoid trouble with constipation: * Orink at teast 8 glasses of water or juice each day unless your Doctor has limited your fluids. * Eat high fiber foods (whole graln breads and cereals, vegetables and fruits). * Call a support program called for suppor in helping you manage through this loss of your pregnancy. * Work with your doctor as you ptan future pregnancies.

Call your doctor if you have:

trouble passing your urine.

Increased bleeding or drainage trom your incisien, swelling, redness or opening of your incision. foul small from your incision or dressing. increased or foul smefiing vagina! discharge. chills or fever over 101 degrees (by mouth). pain not helped by your pain medicine.

pain in your calf er leg.

trouble or pain with breathing.

Increased nausea or vomiting.

This is Information About Your New Medications - Start taking as

Prescribed. eee

IRON SUPPLEMENTS (Ferraus Sulfate - Feosol or Fer-In-Sol, Ferrous Fumarate - Feostat, Ferrous Gtuconate - Fergon, Iron Dextran - DexFerrum , lron Polysaccharide - Niferex, Iron Sucrose - Venofer, others)

Take this medicine by mouth in the following dose: 1 tablet 2 times a day,

This is an iron supplement. Iron |s a mineral that you normally get through the food you eat. Itis used to treat some kinds of anemia (not enough iron in the bload). iron is needed to form healthy red blood cells. This medicine may be used for cther reasons, as prescribed by your doctor.

Side effecta may include:

* diarrhea

* heartburn

* upsel stomach

* constipation

« loss of appetite

Other sida effects may occur, but are not as common. Signs of allergy may Include: facial or throat swelling, rash, wheezing or shortness of breath.

Follow these Instructions:

* Do not take antacids or caicium supplements at the same time as this medicine. Take antacids and calcium supplements 1 hour before or 2 hours after the iran supplement.

* Do not crush sustained release (SR} capsules.

* This medicine works best if you take it on an empty stomach (1 hour before or2 hours aftar meats). If this causes an upset stomach, you may take it with food.

« Expect your bowe! movements to be black in color while taking this iran supplament. This is not harmful.

« Certain foods dacrease this medicine's affect on your body. Do not eat the fotlowing within two hours of the iron supplement:

* eggs * cheese * milk

° coffee * tea

* If you will ba having lab tests while taking this medicine, lel! the person in charge that you are taking an Iron supplement. Iron can affect lab resulta, especially when looking for blood in the bowel movements.

¢ tfyou are taking the liquid solution, mix each dose in water or fruil juice and drink it from a straw. This helps keep the iron off your teeth, sinca liquid iron can stain your teeth.

* Keep all follow-up appointments with your doctor.

* Store this medicine atroom temperature, away from heat, moisture or direct light.

* Store this medicine away from children's reach. This medicine can ba dangerous if a child takes too much of it,

* Ifyou are taking this medicine on a regular schedule and miss adose, take it as soon as you remember it. If it is almnst time for your next dose, skip the missed dose. Do not double the doses.

IBUPROFEN (Motrin, Advil, Rufen, Nuprin)

Take this medicine with food and an eight-ounce glass of water in the following dose: 800 mg by mouth every 8 hours !f needed. WARNING: Do not lake this medicine if you are pregnant.

This medicine is used to reduce pain and inflammation (redness and swelling). itis atso used totreat arthritis, muscie aches, headaches, athletic injuries, gout and menstrual cramps. Ibuprofen also reduces a fever, This medicine may be used for other reasons, as prescribed by your doctor,

Side effects may include:

* an upset stomach or abdominal pain

+ heartburn

¢ dizziness or drowsiness

* abdominal or rectal bleeding

Olher side effects may occur, but are not as common. Allergy would show up as: rash or itching, facial or throat swalling, wheezing or shortness of breath.

Follow these instructions:

* Donattake any other pain or anti-inflammatory medicines (such as aspirin, naproxen, ketoprofen, celecoxib, others) while taking this medicine, (Read the tabels on all non-prescription pain and fever-reducing medicinas.)

» Store this medicine away from heat, moisture or direct light.

* Ifyou miss a dose and are taking it on a regular schedule, take it as soon as possible. [fitis almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose. Do not doubie the doses. If you are taking the axtended-ratease form af this medicine, only take the missed dose if you remember it within 2 hours after you should have taken it,

* Talk with your doctor before taking any other medicines (including vitamins and herbals} as you may require additional monitoring.

Gall your doctor if you have:

* any sign of allargy.

» black, tar-like bowel movements or blood in your vomit (bright red or dark brown that looks like coffee grounds}.

* severe nausea, heartburn or stomach pain.

* confusion.

« skin rash with fever, chilts and muscle aches.

* swelling in your legs and feet.

* unusual weight gain.

: fainting.

+ irreguiar or very fast heartbeat.

+ ‘vision changes or ringing in your ears.

* easy bruising or bleeding.

HYDROCODONE and ACETAMINOPHEN (Vicodin, Vicodin ES, Lortab, Lortab elixir, Zamicet, Norco, Zydone, Anexsia, Anolor, Bancap HC)

Take this medicine by mouth with food in the fallowing dose: one Smg/325mg tablet every 4 to 6 hours if naeded for pain. Do not take mora than 5 tablets per day (24 hours).

This is a mixture of medicines (hydrocodone and acetamingphen) used to raileve moderate to severe pain. This medicine may be used for cther reasons, as prescribed by your doctor.

Side effects may Include:

¢ sleepiness or dizziness

* upset siomach, nausea or vomiting

¢ constipation

Other side effects may occur, but are notas common. Alleray would show up as: rash or itching, facial or throat swelling, wheezing or shortness of breath. This medicine can be habit forming if used for a long period of time.

Faltow these instructlons:

« Never take more of this medicine than prescribed. Too much acetaminophen tn your body can cause liver damage.

* Read the labels of non-prescriptian medicines before taking them. Many contain acetaminophen. To avoid an overdose, do not take any other medicines that contain acetaminophen.

* Talktoyour doctor or pharmacist before taking medicines forsleep, colds or allergies. Severe drowsiness may occur.

* Do not share this medicine with others as this medicine is a controlled-substance. Sharing this medicine with others is against the law.

* To avoid constipation while taking this medicine:

¢ Drink plenty of liquids. Try to drink 8to 10 cight-ounce glasses of water or juice each day.

* Include extra fiber in your diet.

* Exercise dally.

* Watch for signs of dependence:

* feeling that you “cannot live without this medicine”.

* you need mora of this medicine than before to get tha same ratief.

* Be not drink alcohol, drive or qperate machinesy until you know how this medicine affacts you.

Store this medicine away from heat, moisture or direct light.

* If you are taking this on a regular schedule and you miss a dose, take it as soon as possible. Iitis almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose and return to your regular schedule. Do not double the doses.

* Talk with your doctor before taking any other medicines (Including vitamins and herbals) as you may require additional monitoring.

Call your doctor if you have:

any sign of dependence or allergy.

increased pain not helped by the pain medicine. slow, weak breathing.

seizures.

slow or irregular heart beat.

@ yetlow-color to your skin or eyes, or dark urine. stomach pain.

unusual or extreme tiredness.

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco?

* Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Hearl and Lung Disease,

+ Smoking/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hues your family and friends.

What are the Benefits of Quitting?

° Your chances of geting cancer decrease.

* Your chance of heart atlack decreases within 24 hours.

+ You save money and your taste and smell will improve.

How to quit?

* Set a quit date and stick to it-not a single puff more.

« Be sure lo have a strong support group such as: family, friends, co-worker, and your doctor,

+ Eat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself from smoking.

Need Help?

Call Kentucky's Tobacco Quiitine: 1-[PHONE-1309] (1-800-Quit-Now) or your local Health Department.

Healthy Heart Tips

* Eat more grains, starches, frults and vegetables

+ Add as little fat as possible (oil, margarine, mayonnaise, salad dressing, gravies) to your food

Avoid fried foods

Select only lean meats, fish, poultry, in “deck of card” size portions Select only iow-fal cheese

Use skim or 1% milk and dairy products

Limit your daily sodium intake in your diel

Monitor weight daily on the same scale

Develop a regular exercise regimen as tolerated undez your physician's guidelines

significant concerns develop

Global Patient Safety

The staff of Baptist Health Corbin regards your safety as a priority. We take several precautions to assure your safety during you stay. We require all hospital staff to wear photo identification badges, so all our patients know who we are. Therefore, we ask that you follow these guidelines:

Should you leave the floor for personal reasons, please follow these guidelines:

Notify staff when leaving the floor.

Go to the nurses’ station to sign out.

When you return to the floor please sign back in on the log book at the nurses station.

If you have not returned to your room by the time you have given us, we will initiate our “Code Purple” to locate you, to assure your safety. “Code Purple” alerts our staff to search for missing patients.

PON

We thank you for choosing Baptist Health Corbin for your health care needs.

BLOOD TRANSFUSION AND BLOOD PRODUCTS RISKS, BENEFITS, & ALTERNATIVES-If you wish not to receive blood or blood products please request to sign a refusal form. 1) Some surgical procedures may require blood transfusions or blocd products. My doctor has informed me that I may need to have blood products administered during the course of my treatment. 2) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products involves the introduction of the product into one of my veins, commonly in the arm using a needle. I understand that the amount of blood preducts administered is determined by my doctor based upon my particular needs. 3) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products may expose me to certain risks. Minor or temporary reactions, such as slight bruising, swelling, or Jocal infection of the area where the needle pierces the skin is not urrcommon. Non-serious reactions may include headache, fever, or a mild skin reaction, such as itching or a rash. Rare, but serious reactions are possible such as the transmission of diseases including hepatitis and AIDS, which require lengthy treatment and can be fatal. It has been further explained that while procedures for screening blood products for diseases such as AIDS and hepatitis are effective, these procedures are not perfect and there is a risk of exposure to AIDS, hepatitis, or other blood-bome disease. I have taken all of these risks into consideration and have decided that the benefits of receiving blood products outweigh the risks. 4) I have been made aware of the reasonably available alternatives to the administration of blood products and the risks and benefits of those alternatives including autologous blood transfusion, directed donation and not having any blood products administered. I understand that I may be able to donate one of several units of my own blood to be used during planned elective surgery and that my doctor must determine my ability to donate for myself. This is called autologous transfusion.

If I choose to have an autologous transfusion, I understand that the last unit of blood must be donated one week prior to expected use. Talso understand that I may choose a directed donation. A directed donation means I] am allowed to select

ANESTHESIA-I understand that this procedure may require anesthesia. An anesthesiologist has or will explain the anesthesia alternatives, Complications from any type of anesthetic are infrequent, but do occur. Complications are more frequent for patients in poor health than for patients in good health. Complications associated with all types of anesthesia include temporary or permanent damage to teeth, lips, nose, throat, voice box, windpipe, eyes, nerves, or blood vessels. Drug reaction may occur and may be minor or serious even requiring resuscitation, Breathing problems may develop.

Headache, back pain, some degree of awareness, infection, nausea and/or vomiting may occur. There are some very serious complications, which are uncommon, but can occur. These include paralysis, brain damage, or death. There are other potential complications depending on your situation; however, it is not possible to advise you of every imaginable complication. The purpose of this is not to frighten you, but to properly inform you. Serious complications are unlikely. The benefits of surgery and anesthesia, except in extreme circumstances, outweigh the potential risks. 1 agree that the potential benefit of the procedure(s) outweighs the potential risks of anesthesia.

Tt understand that my procedure is scheduled to be done with one of the following:

General Anesthesia-monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist with or without a CRNA

Monitored Anesthesia Care-possible sedation, monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist or a CRNA Regional Anesthesia-monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist or a CRNA Local Anesthesia-care managed by the Physician and monitored by 4 registered nurse Moderate Sedation-care managed by the Physician and monitored by a registered nurse

Deoadoa

I understand that I must be: 1.) Accompanied by a responsible adult.

2.) Not take public transportation without the presence of another adule.

3.) Not drive a car, ingest alcoholic beverages, or depressant medication without direct permission from the physician, for a minimum of 24 hours.

4.) Not make any important decisions or sign any important documents or checks for 24 to 36 hours

There is the possibility that any of the services provided above may convert to a general anesthetic. These same complications may occur with local anesthesia. It may become necessary for the anesthesiologist to render care ina life-threatening emergency situation without my previous consent. If during a local anesthetic an emergent situation arises I understand that my physician may consult with an anesthesiologist to render the necessary emergency medical care.

BAPTIST HEALTH Visit#: 70000129296 [DOB-5211] TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT & SMOKING CESSATION —-998: [DOB-6556] 30y FO MRN: 728945 CAMA AA A A A

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health's campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that 1 am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if i leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | wilt not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation ts quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine Is a chemical that makes you fee! good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me?

e Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer.

* Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs.

* Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. . Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers

" Why is quitting good for me?

As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The langer you do not smoke, the smailer your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit? First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Join a support group for peeple who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who wili quit smoking with you.

Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor.

Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

e e » s . ° * * ° ¢ e « .

MEDICATION: VICODIN (Hydrocodone) VICODIN (generic: Hydrocodone + acetaminophen) is a narcotic pain medicine, —“it dis also available under the trade names LORTAB and ANEXSIA, NORCO.

TRECTIONS FOR USE:

£ this medicine makes your stomach upset, take it with food. Pain medication should be taken only if needed at the times prescribed. If you are not having pain, do not take the medicine, unless you are advised to do so by your doctor. WHAT TO WATCH FOR:

POSSIBLE SIDE EEFECTS: Dizziness, drowsiness -- > (Take a smaller dose; for example, break the pill in half or taka it less often). Constipation -- > (Drink lots of liquids, use small doses of a mild laxative like Milk of Magnesia, as neaded). Nausea, vomiting -- > (Take with food). Difficulty passing urine-- > (Stop the medicine and contact your doctor). ALLERGIC REACTION: Rash, itching, swelling, trouble breathing or swallowing -- > (Contact your doctor or return to this facility promptly).

RKKKKAIKKK TMPORTANT ek tok RK KK MEDICAL CONDITIONS: Before starting this medicine, be sure your doctor knows if you have any of the following conditions:

-- Prostate enlargement

-- Pregnancy or breast feeding

DRUG INTERACTIONS: This drug may cause increased side effects when taken with alcohol, muscle relaxant, sedative, tricyclic antidepressant, MAO-inhibitor or another pain medicine. WARNINGS :

~~ DO NOT DRIVE, ride a bicycle or operate dangerous equipment while taking this medicine until you know how it will affect you.

-- Prolonged use of this medicine can be HABIT FORMING and may lead to ADDICTION.

(NOTE: This information topic may not include all directions, precautions, medical conditions, drug/food interactions and warnings for this drug. Check with your doctor, nurse or pharmacist for any questions that you may have. ]

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco? Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease. + Smoking/Tobacce use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends. What are the Benefits of Quitting? * Your chances of getting cancer decreases. ‘ * Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours. * You save money and your taste and smell will improve. How to quit? * Set a quit date and stick to it-not a single puff more. ' Be sure to have a strong support group such as: family, friends, co-worker, and your doctor. * Bat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself from noking. asd Help? Call Kentucky's Tobacco Quitline: 1-[PHONE-1405] (1-800-Quit-Now) or your local Health Department. Smoking/Tobacco Cessation Counseling.

This is for educational purposes only, If you have any questions, please discuss them with your nurse. The receipt of this form will NOT affect your insurance benefits or hospital bill.

Why Should You Stop Smoking or Using Tobacco? Smoking/Tobacco use causes CANCER to grow. Smoking/Tobacco use causes HEART and LUNG DISEASE. Smoking/Tobacco use is EXPENSIVE.

Smoking/Tobacco use quickens the AGING process. Second-hand smoke HURTS your family and friends. Smoking damages TASTEBUDS and discolors your TEETH and NAILS. Smoking increases NERVOUSNESS.

What are the Benefits of Quitting?

Your chances of getting cancer decrease.

Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours. YOU SAVE MONEY!

Your taste and smell will improve.

You will have more energy.

= You will live longer! How to Quit: Ready — Set a quit date and stick to it —not a single puff more.

Set ~ Get support from family, friends, co-workers, doctor, or other health care providers — get group, individual, or telephone counseling.

Go — Learn new ways to live healthy:

= Change your routine to distract yourself from smoking.

« Reduce stress.

= Eat a healthy diet and stay active.

= Drink plenty of water and other fluids. Medication That Can Help: Falk to your healthcare provider about whether medication is right for you. Common medications available to help you quit:

« ZYBAN — www. zyban. com — available by prescription

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE INDEPENDENT PRACTITIONERS:

In mest situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is provided by physicians who are independent practitioners and not employees or agents of the Hospital. Independent practitioners may include, but are not limited to, anesthesiclogists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency room physicians, pathologists, surgeons and surgical assistants. There are also other health care providers in the Hospital who are not employees or agents of the Hospital Including, but not limited to certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, Physician assistants, and psychologists. You will receive a separate bill for these services or the Hospital bill you receive will have separate charges for services of physicians and other Independent practitioners, Charges for these services are established by the physicians and other independent practitioners.

3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:

| authorize the Hospital and the independent practitioners on its medical staff to use and disclose Information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be flable for paying any claim fer benefits arising out of services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital; (b) any independent practitioner or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home haalth agencies, nursing homes, and physiclans: (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; and (e) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices, This consent includes the telease of medical records and billing information related to drug-related congitions, alccholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne Infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | alsa authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.

POSSIBLE MISCARRIAGE [Threatened Abortion]

During early pregnancy (first three months), it is not uncommon to have a

amall amount of bleeding. This can be entirely normal, But if heavy bleeding > severe cramping occur, it can be an sarly sign of miscarriage. A iiscarriage" means unexpected loss of your pregnancy.

an about half of patients with bleeding or cramping during early pregnancy,

these symptoms will step and the pregnancy will continue normally. However,

half of the time a miscarriage will occur. A miscarriage may occur due to

various causes. These include urinary or pelvic infection, abnormal growth of

the fetus, blow to the lower abdomen (stomach). In most cases no cause can be

found. Be reassured that this is not the result of anything that you did

wrong, and it will not interfere with your ability to become pregnant in the

future.

HOME CARE: To improve the chance of keeping this pregnancy, you should do the

following:

1) Rest in bed until the pain and bleeding stop.

2) Avoid setual intercourse for the next 3 weeks.

3) Deo not doushe or use tampons.

FOLLOW-UP: Make an appointment with your doctor within the next week, or as

directed by our staff.

(NOTE: If you had an ultrasound, it will be reviewed by a specialist. You

will be notified of any new findings that may affect your care.]

GET PROMPT MEDICAL ATTENTION if any of the following occur:

-- Vaginal bleeding or pain for more than three days

-- Heavy bleeding ({seaking one pad an hour for three hours)

-- Fever over 100.0° F (37.8° G)

-- Worse pain

-- Weakness, dizziness or fainting

-~- Passage of anything that resembles tissue: pink or grayish membrane or

solid material (save and bring to the doctor)

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco? Smoking/Tobacco usa can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease. Smoking/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends. What are the Benefits of Quitting? * Your chances of getting cancer decrease. * Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours. * You save money and your taste and smell will improve. How to quit? ' Set a quit date and stick to it-not a single puff more. * Be sure to have a strong support group such as: family, friends, co-worker, and your doctor. ' Bat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself from smoking. Need Help? all Kentucky's Tobacco Quitline: 1-[PHONE-5050] (1-800-Quit-Now) or your 2Gal Health Department. whitley County Health Pept: [PHONE-5049] (Williamsburg) [PHONE-5048] (Corbin) Laurel County Health Dept: [PHONE-5047], Knox County Health Dept: 606-546~3486, American Heart Association [PHONE-1511], American Lung Association (706)

2, PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

In most situations, the medical! treatment provided to patients in the Haspital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Hospital. Such providers include, but are not timited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by these providers.

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies ta all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health's campus or at any facility owned, teased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that! will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or Interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed,

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn’t there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me? Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer.

* Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. * S§moking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers

Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down.

The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

what is the best way to quit? First, decide In your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk ta ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you.

Before yaur quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and peopie that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes,

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor.

Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

Thank you for choosing Baptist Health! You can view your lab results (36 hours aiter discharge), discharge instructions, a lisi of procedures performed and other details of your visit online using our secure website, My Baptist Health KyY.com. Check your email for your personal invitatton, and click on the My Bantist Health tink to gat slarted. Questions? Please call Health Information Management at [PHONE-1745].

The failowing infarmation wlll help you return to your best level of health, Everyone's recovery Is different.

Normal Discharge Home This information Is About Your Follow Up Care

Your recovery requires a follow up appointment with Travis Gilbert, MD. Keep your scheduled appoiniment. You can reach Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-1746], 1 Trilium Way Suite 200, Corbin, KY, 40701, If you have any problems or concerns bafore your appointment call the clinic,

This Information Is About Procedures

NON-STRESS TEST

Anon-stress test is done tomeasure how your baby's heart rate responds to its own movements, Healthy babfes' heart rates increase when they move, then decrease when they rest. This is called a “reactive stress test". When a baby is moving around, adequate blood supply and oxygen is needed for the baby's heart rate to increase. When the oxygen levels are iow, a baby’s heart rate may nat respond normally to movement. Low oxygen levels can often ve caused by problems with the placenta or umbllical cord.

We will be place a strap around your abdomen that holds two monitors. One monitor will check for and measure contractions of your uterus. The other monitor will measure your baby’s heartbeat. This testis meantio be dane when your baby is not under any stress. The test usually lakes 20 to 30 minutes.

ihe baby's heart rate does not increase atleast twice with mavement within 20 minutes, we call it a "non-reactive non-stress test", This does not mean there is a problem. Further testing may be needed.

You may return to your usual activities as directed by your healthcare provider.

Contact your healthcare provider if:

* your water breaks.

» you have bleeding from your vagina that is more than spotting. * you have constanl, severe abdominal! pain.

+ you have strong, regular contractions 5 minutes apart or less. * you have any new or unusual symptoms.

This information Is About Your tilness and Dlagnesis

ANTENATAL; Today yau have come to the hospital for observation.. The results of this visit seém notmal and you may go home.

Other instructions: * Drink plenty of fluids * Lie on your sides, * Keep bladder empty. + Do not smoke. if you smoke, please try nat to smoke for at least 2 hours befora coming for your next test. Smoking changes the test results.

Movement of the baby: * Anytime you think your baby isn't moving as much as il should; you need to count the bables movements in the following manner:

Choose a 1 hour time period, during the time your baby Is most active. Lay down on your side, Count each time the baby kicks, rolls over or moves in any way. The baby should move at least 6 times, in a hour. the baby does not move six times in one hour,then come to the hospital, Go to labor hall and let the nurse know that your baby is not moving as it should.

Return to the hospital If you have any of the following:

* Water leaks or breaks.

* Vaginal bleeding more than a period or any bright red bleeding. Contractlons that progressively get stronger, last longer- and get closer

together that are not relieved by walking or resting on your sides.

- Experience severe, sharp, or rigid abdomen (belty) pain that is continuous.

Gall your Doctor if you have:

+ Any new problems or concerns.

This is Information About Your New Medications - Start taking as prescribed.

HYDROXYZINE (Atarax, Vistaril)

Take this medicine in the following dose: 50 mg by mouth every 6 hours AS NEEDED. if needed,

This medicine Is used to relieve nausea and itching during pregnancy. It also helps people sleep. Hydroxyzine may be used for other reasons, as prescribed by your doctor.

Side effects may include:

- sleepiness

* excitability

* dry mouth

Other side effects may occur, but are not as common. Allergy would show up as: rash or itching, facial or throat swelling, wheezing or shortness of breath,

Follow these instructions:

* Use gum, hard candy, or Ice chips to relieve a dry mouth,

Read the labels on non-prescription medicines before taking hem. Many contain ingredients that also cause drowsiness.

Use caution when driving while taking this medicine.

Store this medicine away from heat, moisture, or direct tight.

Ifyou miss a dose, take It as soon as possible. Ifitis almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose, Do not double the doses.

Talk with your doctor before taking any other medicines (including herbals) as you may require additional monitoring especially:

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco?

+ Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease.

+ Smoking/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurls your family and friends.

What are the Benefits of Quitting?

« Your chances of getting cancer decreasa.

+ Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours.

« You save money and your taste and smell wil! improve.

How to quit?

+ Set a quit date and stick to it-not a single puff more.

* Ge sure to have a strong support group such as: family, friends, co-worker, and your doctor.

* Eat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself from smoking.

Need Help?

Call Kentucky’s Tabacco Quitline: 1-[PHONE-1749] (1-800-Quit-Now} or your local Health Department.

Whitley County Health Dept: [PHONE-1750] (Williamsburg) [PHONE-1751] (Corbin)

Laurel County Health Dept: [PHONE-4805], Knox County Health Dept: [PHONE-1753],

List:

Healthy Heart Tips

» Eat more grains, starches, fruits and vegetables

+ Add as little fat as possible (oil, margarine, mayonnaise, salad dressing, gravies) to your food

" Avoid friad foods

* Select only lean meats, fish, poultry, in "deck of card” size portions

+ Select only low-fat cheese

+ Use skim or 1% milk and dairy products

+ Limit your daily sodium intake in your diet

* Monitor weight daily on the same scale

+ Develop a regular exercise regimen as tolerated under your physician's guidelines

+ Take all prescribed medications as directed by your physician

* See your physician regularly for routine check-ups and contact them if any significant concerns develop

- Ifyou have been diagnosed wih Heart Failure and notice swelling of enkles/feet or have a two pound weight gain overnight, contact your physician.

Stroke Prevention and Risk Factors * Overweight

« Sedentary Lifestyle

* Smoking

« High Blcod Pressure

+ Diabetes

+ Poor Diet

+ Heart Disease

= Family History

+ Carotid Artery Disease

* Increased Alcahe! Consumption » Prior Stroke of TIA

Warning Signs of a Stroke include but are not iimited to: + Sudden numbness or weakness of arms or legs

* Slurred speech or unable to understand speech

« Headache like you have never had before

* Trouble seeing from ane eye or both

« Trouble watking, loss of coordinalion, or dizziness

« Sudden canfusion

Calt 841 immediately if you notice any of these warning signs.
