2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Hospital. Such providers inciude, but are not limited to, anesthesiologisis, cardiologists, radiotogists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be emptoyed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by these providers.

3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:

| authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports weliness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus enviranment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited fram use on Baptist Health's campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed, | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | feave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why itis so important to stop smoking, Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need te smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me?

¢ Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer.

* Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs.

* Smoking ralses your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. * Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers

Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The longer you do not smoke, the smatler your risk for these diseases and problems.

What Is the best way to quit? First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk to ¢x-smokers about what helped them quit.

Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you.

Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make a list of ail of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor.

Throw away alt cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

DISCHARGE JNSTRUCTIONS

Thank you for choosing Baptist Health! You can view your lab results (36 hours after discharge}, discharge Instructions, a list of Pproceduses performed and other details of your visit online using our secure website, My Baptist Health KY¥.com. The following Information will he!p you return to your best leve! of health. Everyone's recovery is different.

Normal Discharge Home This Information Is About Your Follow Up Care

Your recovery requires a follow up appointment with Travis Gilbert, MD. Keep your scheduled appointment. You can reach Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-1806], 1 Trillium Way Sulte 200, Corbin, KY, 40701. If you have any problems or concems before your appointment call the clinic.

This Information is About Procedures

NON-STRESS TEST

Anon-stress test is done to measure how yourbaby's heart rate responds te its own mavemnents. Healthy babies‘ heart rates increase when they move, then decrease when they rest. This is called 4 "reactive stress test". When a baby is moving around, adequate blood supply and oxygen is needed for the baby's heart rate to increase. When the oxygen levels are low, a baby’s heart rate may not respand normally to mavement. Low oxygen levels can often be caused by problems with the placenta or umbilical cord,

We will be place a strap around your abdomen that holds two monitors. One monitor will check for and measure contractions of your uterus. The ofher monilor will measure your baby’s heartbeat. This test is meant ic be done when your baby is not under any stress. The test usually takes 20 to 30 minutes.

Ifthe baby's heart rate does not increase at least twice with movement within 20 minutes, we callita "non-reactive non-stress test". This does nol mean there is @ problem. Further testing may be needed.

You may return to your Usual activities as directed by your healthcare provider.

Contact your healthcare provider if:

« your water breaks.

« you have bleeding from your vagina that is more than spotting. " you have constant, severe abdominat pain.

* you have strong, regular contractions 5 minutes apart or less. * you have any new or unusual symptoms.

Other Instructions: » Drink plenty of fluids ¢ Lie on your sides. « Keep bladder empty. * Do not smoke. If you smoke, please try not to smoke for at least 2 hours before coming for your next test. Smoking changes the test results.

Movement of the baby; * Anything you think your baby isn't moving as much as it should; you need to count the babies movements in the following manner:

Choose a 1 hour time period, during the time your baby is most active. Lay down on your side, Gount each time the baby kicks, rolls over or moves In any way. The baby should move at least 6 times, in a hour.If the baby does not move six times in one hour,then come to the hospital. Go to labor hall and let the nurse know that your baby Is net moving as it should.

+ Water leaks or breaks.

together that are not relieved by walking or resting on your sides.

+ Eat more grains, starches, fruits and vegetables

+ Add as little fat as possible (oll, margarine, mayonnaise, salad dressing, gravies) ta your food

° Avold fried foods

* Select only lean meats, fish, poultry, in “deck of card” size portions

+ Limit your daily sodium intake In your diet

+ Monitor weight daily on the same scale

* Develop a regular exercise regimen as tolerated under your physician's guidelines

* Take all prescribed medications as directed by your physiclan

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

in most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Hospital. Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers, Charges for such services are established by these providers.

3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:

| authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures include, but are not limited to, providing Information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMQs, Insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third partly administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital; (b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home heaith agencies, nursing homes, and physicians; {d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; and (e) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital's Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review Purposes. | also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.

BAPTIST HEALTH Visit#: 70000243836 [DOB-4738] TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT & SMOKING CESSATION 908: [DOB-6556] 31y_ F MRN: 728945 UNTIECAE UNO ARTE A RED EG AD

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacce Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health’s campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Weliness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking Is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your tungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Doparnine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it Is so important to stop smaking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn’t there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me?

* Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer. * Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. . Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The tonger you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit?

First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are availabie. Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you.

Before yaur quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by nat buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor.

Throw away ail cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

Thank you for choosing Baptist Health! You can view your lab results (36 hours after discharge), discharge instructions, a list of procedures performed and other details of your visit anline using our secure website, My Baptist Health KY.com. Check your emall for your personal invitallon, and click on the My Baptist Health link to get started. Questions? Please call Health infarmation Management at [PHONE-1979].

The fatlowing information wilt help you return to your best level of health, Everyone's recovery Is differant.

Normal Discharge Home This Information Is About Your Follow Up Care

Your recovery requires a follow up appointment with Travis Gilbert, MD. Keep your scheduled appointment. You can reach Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-1980], * Trillium Way Sulte 200, Corin, KY, 40701. If you have any problems or concerns before your appointment call the clinic.

This Information Is About Procedures

NON-STRESS TEST

Anon-stress test is done to measure how your baby's heart rate responds to its own movements. Healthy babies’ heart rates increase when they move, then decrease when they rest. This is called a "reactive stress test". When a baby is moving around, adequate blood supply and oxygen is needed for the baby's heart rate to increase. Whan the oxygen levels are low, a baby's heart rate may hol respand normally to movement. Low oxygen levels can often be caused by problems with the placenta or umbilical cord.

We wil! be place a strap around your abdomen that holds two monitors. One monitor wilt chack for and measure contractions of your uterus. The other monitor will measure your baby’s heartbeat. This test is meantto be done when your baby is not under any stress. The test usually takes 20 to 30 minutes.

Ifthe baby's heart rate does not increase at least twice with movement within 20 minutes, we cailit a "non-reactive non-stress test". This does not mean there Is a problem, Further testing may be needed.

You may return to your usual activities as directed by your healthcare provider.

Contact your healthcare provider if:

* your water breaks.

* you have bleeding from your vagina that is mare than spotting, * you have constant, severe abdominal pain.

* you have strong, regular contractions 5 minutes apait or less. + you have any new or unusual symptoms.

This Information ts About Your Illness and Diagnosis

PRE-TERM LAGOR (Early Labor).

The due date for your pregnancy is [DOB-4576]. Regular contractions of your uterus more than a 4 weeks before your due date is called Pre-Term Labor.

Many things can cause early labor. We often don't know the exact cause. Early labor can be risky for your baby. You must do all that you can to avoid it, and treat it early.

Things to Avoid:

EAA

70000252997

* Lying on your back{ always lie on your side). This increases the bload flaw to your uterus,

* Standing for long periods of time.

Lifting heavy things or doing heavy work, playing in an active sport or

frequently climbing stairs.

Long car trips.

Sexual activity af any type, including intercourse.

Full bladder

SMOKING and second hand smoke,

Special Instructions:

* Self Monitoring for pre-term labor:

« Lledown on ieft side. Place fingertips on uterus with one hand on each side of the uterus. If the uterus tigntens or becomes hard, note time and write it down. Note time and how long it fasts.

* Ifyou feel you are having contractions do the following:

1. Empty your bladder.

2. Increase fluids by mouth, itis best to drink water.

3, Lay down on either side for 30 minutes to 4 hour.

4, Make sure you have taken your pill for preterm labor, if it has been longer than 2 hours take a extra pill.

If you are still having contractions, occuring more than 4 an hour.

Go to the hospital.

* Watch for the following symptoms of preterm jabor:

* Increasing contractions (tight feeling in your belly or pelvis).

Blood or other fluid fram your vagina,

Low Backache. Not relieved by change in position.

Low pelvic pressure, (feels like baby is going to "fat! out")

Mensttua!-like cramps.

Intestinal cramps. ("gas palns") May or may not be acconipanied with

diarrhea.

REMEMBER: PRE-TERM LABOR CONTRACTIONS ARE OFTEN

PAINLESS

Activities:Activity:

Call Your doctor if you have:

* any signs of preterm labor listed above.

* chills or fever.

* any new problems or concerns.

* ewe

ee #6 we

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco?

« Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease.

« Smoklng/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends.

What are the Benefits of Quitting?

* Your chances of getting cancer decrease.

* Your chance of heart atlack decreases within 24 hours,

« You save money and your taste and smell will improve.

How to quit?

* Set a quit date and stick to it-not a single puff more.

* Be sure to hava a strong support group such as: family, friends, co-worker, and your doctor,

+ Eat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself from smoking,

Need Help?

Calt Kentucky's Tobacco Quitline: 1-[PHONE-1982] { 1-800-Qult-Now)} or your local Health Depariment.

Healthy Heart Tips

‘ €at more grains, starches, fruits and vegetables

* Add as little fat as possible (vil, margarine, mayonnaise, salad dressing, gravies) to your food

* Avoid fried foods

* Select only lean meats, fish, poultry, in “deck of card" size partions

» Select only low-fat cheese

+ Use skim or 1% milk and dairy products

* Limit your daily sodium intake in your diet

+ Monitor welght daily on the same scale

+ Develop a regular exercise regimen as tolerated under your physician's guldelines

* Take all prescribed medications as directed by your physician

* See your physician regutarty for routine check-ups and contact them if any significant concerns develop

+ if you have been diagnosed with Heart Failure and notice swelling of ankles/feet or have a two pound weight gain overnight, contact your physician.

Stroke Prevention and Risk Factors + Overvelght

* Sedentary Lifestyle

* Smoking

+ High Biood Pressure

+ Diabetes

* Poor Diet

* Heart Disease

* Family History

- Carotid Artery Disease

+ Increased Alcohol Consumption + Prior Stroke or TIA

Warning Signs of a Strake Include but are not Jimited to: + Sudden numbness or weakness of arms or legs

* Slurred spaech or unable to understand speech

* Headache like you have never had before

* Trouble seging fram ons eye or both

* Trouble walking, loss of coordination, or dizziness

Penicillin G, ampicillin, or cefazolin are indicated for intrapartum prophylaxis af perinatal group B Strep (GBS) colonization. Reflex susceptibility testing should be performed prior to use of clindamycin only on GBS Lsolates from penicillin-allergic women who are considered a high risk for anaphylaxis. DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

Thank you for choasing Baptist Health! You can view your lab results (36 hours after discharge), discharge instructions, a list of procedures performed and other details of your visit online using our secure website, My Bapiist Health KY.com. Chack your email for your personat Invitation, and click on the My Baptist Health link to get started. Questions? Please call Health Information Management at [PHONE-2180],

The following Information will help you return to your best level! of health. Everyone's recovery is different,

Narmal Discharge Home This Information ts About Your Follow Up Care

Your recovery requires a follow up appointment with Travis Gilbert, MD, Keep your scheduled appointment. You can reach Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-2181], 1 Trillium Way Suite 200, Corbin, KY, 40701. If you have any problems or concerns before your appoiniment call the clinic.

This (nformatign ls About Procedures

NON-STRESS TEST

Anon-stress test is done to measure how your baby's hear rate responds to its own movements. Healthy bables' heart rates increase when they move, then decrease when they rest, This is calied a “reactive stress test". When a baby is moving around, adequate blood supply and oxygen is needed for the baby's heart rate to increase. When the oxygen levels are (ow, a baby’s heart rate may notrespond normally to movement. Low oxygen levels can often be caused by problems with the placenta or umbilical cord.

We will be place a strap around your abdomen that holds twa monitors. One monitor will check far and measure contractions of your uterus. The other monitor will measure your baby’s heartbeat. This teat is meant to be dona when your Saby is not under any stress. The test usually takes 20 to 30 minutes.

lithe baby's heart rate does not increase at least twice with movement within 20 minutes, we callit a "non-reactive non-stress test”. This does not mean there is 4 problem, Further testing may be needed.

You may return to your usual activities as directed by your healthcare provider.

Contact your healthcare provider éf:

* your water breaks.

* you have bleeding from your vagina thai [s more than spotting. : you have constant, severe abdominal pain.

* you have strong, regular contractions 5 minutes apart or less. * you have any new or unusual symptoms.

This Information Is About Your Iliness and Olagnosis

ANTENATAL;

Today you have come to the hospitaf to show your baby's respanse to fetal movement using a felal monitor.. The results of this visit seem notmal and you may go home.

Do the following:

Activity: Return to your usual activities

Other Instructions: + Drink plenty of fluids + Cie on your sides. * Keep bladder empty. « Do not smoke. lf you smoke, please try not to smoke for at least 2 hours before coming for your next test. Smoking changes the test results.

Movement of the baby: * Anytime you think your baby Isn't moving as much as if should; you need to count the babies movements in the fallowing manner:

Choose a 1 hour time period, during the time your baby is most active. Lay down on your side, Count each time the baby kicks, rolls over or moves in any way. The baby should move at least 6 times, in a hour,!f the baby does not move six times in one hour,then come to the hospital. Go to labor hall and fet the nurse know that your baby is not moving as It should.

Return to the hospltat jf you have any of the following:

+ Water ieaks or breaks.

+ Vaginal bleeding more than a period or any bright red bleeding. Contractions that progressively gel stranger, last longer- and get closer

together that are not relieved by walking or resting on your sides.

+ Experience severe, sharp, or rigid abdomen (belly) pain that is continuaus,

Call your Doctor Hf you have:

» Any new preblems or concerns.

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco?

» Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease.

* Smoking/Tobacco use Is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends.

What are the Benefits of Quitting?

- Your chances of getting cancer decrease.

« Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours.

« You save money and your taste and smeil will improve.

How to quit?

» Set a quit data and stick to it-not a single puff more.

- Be sure to have a strong support group such as; family, friends, co-worker, and your doctor.

+ Eat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself fram smoking.

Healthy Heart Tips

+ Eat more grains, starches, fruits and vegetables

» Add as little fat as possible (ail, margarine, mayonnaise, salad dressing, gravies} to your food

+ Avold fried foods

* Select only lean meats, fish, poultry, in “deck of card" size portions

+ Select only low-fat cheese

+ Use skim or 1% milk and dairy products

* Limit your daily sodium intake in your diet .

+ Monitor weight daily an the same scale

* Develop a regular exercise regimen as tolerated under your physician's guidelines

* Take all prescribed medications as directed by your physician

* See your physician regularly for routine check-ups and contact them if any

significant cancerns develop

* Ifyou have been diagnosed with Heart Failure and notice swelling of ankles/teet or have a two pound welght gain overnight, contact your physician.

Stroke Prevention and Risk Factors > Overweight

+ Sedentary Lifestyle

* Smoking

+ High Blood Pressure

- Diaketes

+ Poor Diet

« Heart Disease

+ Family History

» Carotid Artery Disease

* Increased Alcohol Consumption + Prior Stroke or TIA

Warning Signs of a Stroke include but are not limited to: + Sudden numbness or weakness of arms or legs

* Slurred speech or unable io understand speech

* Headache like you have never had before

2 PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Hospital. Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologlsts, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetisis, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or ihe Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. BAPTIST HEALTH Visit#: 70000256586 [DOB-4365] TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT & SMOKING CESSATION = 2B: 12/28/1083 31y_~F MRN: 726045 FIRE 08 WE SEO LOE NO

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health's campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times, | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | wilt not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician. SMOKING CESSATION Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke}, or these who quit smoking and failed. Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people, The chernical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chernical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn’t there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult. Why is smoking bad for me?

* Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, 4 stroke and/or cancer.

° Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and ether organs.

* Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body.

* Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit?

* First, decide in your mind that you are going ta quit. * Seta specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date. ¢ Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. * Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit. * Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. * — Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you. ¢ Before your quit date, only altow yourself to smoke in one or two places, + Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking. * Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. ° Make a list of all of the goad things that will happen if you quit smoking. * — Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes. . Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor. * Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date. | have receiyed the above i atic paptist Health’s Tobacco Free Environment and smoking cessation. LABOR AND DELIVERY GROUP B STREPTOCOCCAL PROPHYLAXIS ORDERS

ALLERGIES: WEIGHT:

HEIGHT:

Perinata! Group B Streptococcus (GBS ) Prophylaxis Indicated if ANY of the following conditions are met:

Positive GBS screening culture (vaginal and/or rectal) at 35-37 weeks gestation (unless a planned cesarean delivery, in the absence of labor or amniotic membrane rupture, is performed) OR Previous infant with invasive GBS disease OR GBS bacteriuria during current pregnancy OR

Unknown GBS status (culture not done, incomplete, or resutts unknown) and ANY of the following conditions apply:

¢ Delivery at less than 37 weeks gestation

« Amniotic membrane rupture greater than 18 hours

e Intrapartum temperature 100.4 F or greater

Medications: (check desired lines}, Begin on admission. Non PCN/ B-lactam Allergic patients: Ampicillin 2 gm IV x 1 dose, then 1 gm IV every 4 hours until delivery. Penicillin G 5 million units IV x 1 dose, then 2.5 million units IV every 4 hours until delivery.

PCN/B-lactam allergic patients WITHOUT high risk of anaphylaxis: Cefazolin 2 gm IV x 1 dose, then 1 gm IV every 8 hours untit delivery.

PCN/B-lactam allergic patients WITH high risk for anaphylaxis or B-lactarn resistant GBS: Vancomycin 1 gm IV every 12 hours until delivery. If renal insufficiency, vancomycin IV per pharmacy dosing until delivery.

Antibiotic Allergy Assessment Protocol for B- lactams (Penicillins/Cephalosporins): Consider the nature of the reaction:

Patient is considered to have NO allergy to B- lactams if:

No B-lactam allergy history.

Has a history of rash with a drug but has tolerated other B-lactam (penicillins or cephalosporins) antibiotics.

Has a histery of unknown teactions but has tolerated other B-lactam antibiotics.

Patient is considered to have a TRUE allergy to B- lactams if: 4.

Has a history of anaphylaxis or respiratory reactions (hypotension, wheezing, respiratory complications, or iaryngeal/face/lip edema or swelling).

Has a history of severe (hard, raised, or painful) rash and HAS NOT tolerated other B- lactam antibiotics since.

Has a history of unknown reactions and HAS NOT tolerated other B-lactam antibiotics since.

DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

Thank you for chaosing Baptist Health! You can view your lab results (36 hours &fter discharge), discharge instructions, a tist of procedures performed and other details of your visit online using our secure website, My Baptist Health KY.com. Check your email for your personal invitation, and click on the My Baptist Health link to get started. Questions? Please cal! Health Information Management at [PHONE-3797],

The following information will help you return to your best level of health. Everyone's recovery is differenl,

Normal Discharge Home

This Information is About Your Follow Up Care

An appointment has been made for you on [DOB-3798] at 10:00 am to see Travis Gilbert,MD. You can reach Dr. Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-3799], 1 Trillium Way Suite 200, Corbin, KY, 40701.

This Information ts About Procedures

VAGINAL DELIVERY. EYour baby was born through the vaginal opening. You can expect to return to your usual diet, activity and body functions within 5-6 weeks.

Foliow these instructions: ~ Do not liff, pull, push or strain with heavy things. Oo not resume or begin exercise programs until okay with your doctor. Rest often throughout your day. wear a bra and girdle for the next 5-6 weeks. After each time you urinate or pass stoo!:

* Wash the area well with water and pat dry front to back,

° Use Tucks and/or the spray on your bottom until the discharge

has stopped.

Take SITZ BATHS 2-3 times aday. You may do this using yourbathtub. The moist heat will help you heal and relax. You might feel tightening and pulling of the stitches. These will dissolve in 7-10 days. Your vaginal discharge will continue for about 2-3 weeks. tt wilt gradually lighten to a white color, Da not use tampons or douche. Do not have sexual intercourse until it is okay with your doctor. Do not use extra salt for the next 2 weaks. To prevent constipation:

Orink atleast & glasses of water or juice each day unless your doctor has limited your fluids.

* Eathigh fiber foods (whole grain breads and cereals, vegetables and fruits).

*ee#e

e e

Call your doctor if you have:

chills or fever over 101 degrees (by mouth).

increasing pain, bleeding with clots (more than 4 pad in 1 hour) increasing discharge or a foul sme to the discharge.

mood swings that are persistent and interrupt your daily living.

any cracks, redness, Increasing pain of your nipples with breast feeding.

70000259923

RREKAS 360 of 862

MAA

70000259923

* any new problems or concerns. This Information Is About Your Illness and Diagnosis

CHILD&SIRTH, MOTHER CARE You have had a baby. Itis important to take care of yourself as well as the baby in order to feel better.

Post-partum dlues:;

Many women will experience a form of depression. This ls generally seen about three days after the birth and can last about two weeks. This is caused by the sudden change in your body's harmones, plus the added fife changes of anew baby. The norma! reactions for thls are: crying, irritability, anger, exhaustion, tension, restlessness, and anxlety.

Changes of your uterus:

Your uterus will continue to shrink to its non-pregnant size. You may get your menstrual period in three to eight weeks if you are not breast-feeding, Ifyou are breast-feeding, you may not get your period for six months or longer. You will usuaily ovulate before you gat your period. You may even ovulate without a period. lf you do not want another pregnancy, use some form of birth contro}. Your first period may be much heavier than normal, but your second period should be closer to normal.

Birth control:

If you de not want another pregnancy immediately, use some type of birth control. You can use spermicidal foam and condoms, Talk with your health care provider about the specific birth contro! that would be right for you.

Care of your bottom: Always wipe from front to back after using the bathroom. Change your pads

often to prevent irritation. Do not use tampons until your health care provider says itis okay (usually 6 to 8 weeks), You may shower or take a bath. Donol use bubble bath if you take a tub bath.

Care gf hemorrhoids:

Try using moist lowelettes or medicated towelettes, such as Tucks(TM). Take stool softeners if prescribed. Eat ptenty of fruits, vegetables and whole grain breads and cereals. Drink eight 8-ounce glasses of water each day.

Vagina! discharge:

You will have some vaginal discharge for the next four to six weeks, It is the debris that the uterus sheds after defivery, The discharge will change start out red and change to pinkish to yellow to white. Ht will increase with activity or breastieeding.

Sexual intercourse: You may fesume intercourse after 6 weeks, Que to hormonal changes, your

vagina may be drier than usual, To increase comfart, try using a lubricant, such as K-Y Jelly{TM),

Please follow these Instructions:

» Rest oflen. Try to nap when your baby is napping.

* Ask for help from others.

» Talk about your feelings with a friend or family member,

* Exercise regulady when your health care provider says it's okay. * Take time far yourself,

* Oo not lift anything heavier than your baby.

+ Do not drive for 2 weeks.

Contact your health care provider as soon as possible if you have any of tha fotlowing:

* depression or "blues" that make it hard for you to care for your baby. = feelings of hapslessnéss.

= Intense sleepiness.

* feelings to harm your baby.

* feelings that you want to hurt yourself,

This is Information About Your New Medications - Start taking as Prescribed.

*e* @ @ aie

HYDROCODONE AND ACETAMINOPHEN (Vicodin, Vicodin ES, Lortab, Lortab elixir, Zamicet, Norco, Zydone, Anexsia, Anotar, Bancap HC)

Take this medicine by mouth with food in the following dose: one 5mg/325mg tablet every 4 to 6 hours if needed for pain. Do not take more than 5 tablets per day (24 hours).

This Is a mixture of medicines (hydrocodone and acetaminophen) used ta relieve moderate to severe pain. This medicine may be used for other reasons, as prescribed by your dactar.

Side effects may include:

* sleepiness or dizziness

* upsei stomach, nausea, or vomiting

* constipation

Other side effects may occur, but are not as common. Allergy would show up as: rash or itching, facial or throat swelling, wheezing or shortness of breath. This medicine can be habit forming if used for a long period of time.

At home, please fotlow these instructions: + Never take more of this medicine than prescribed. Too much acetaminophen in your body can cause liver damage. Read the labels of non-prescription medicines before taking them. Many contain acetaminaphen, To avoid an overdose, do not take any other medicines that contain acetaminophen. This medicine may make you dizzy ar drowsy. There is a higher chance you could fall. To prevent falling: + Stand up slowly. if you feel dizzy, haid on to something sturdy until the feeling goes away. * Do not do any activities that require alertness until you know you can safely do so. » Use plenty of light in your house. + Reduce clutler in your home.

IBUPROFEN (Motrin, Advil, Rufen, Nuprin)

Take this medicine with faod and an eight-ounce giass of water in the fallowing dase: GOO mg by mouth every 6 hours If needed,

WARNING: Do not take this medicine if you are pregnant,

This medicine is used to reduce pain and inflammation (redness and swelling). Itis aiso used to treat arthritis, muscle aches, headaches, athletic injuries, gout and menstrual cramps, Ibuprofen also reduces a fever. This medicine may be used for other reasons, as prescribed by your doctor.

Side effects may include:

* an upset stomach or abdominal pain

* heartburn

+ dizziness or drowsiness

* abdominal or rectal bleeding

Other side effects may occur, but ara not as common. Allergy would show up as: rash or itching, facial or throat swelling, wheezing or shortness of breath.

Global Patient Safety

The staff of Baptist Health Corbin regards your safety as a priority. We take several precautions to assure your safety during you stay, We require all hospital staff to wear photo identification badges, so all our patients know who we are. Therefore, we ask that you follow these guidelines:

Should you ieave the floor for personal reasons, please follow these guidelines:

Notify staff when leaving the floor.

Go to the nurses’ station to sign out.

When you return to the floor please sign back in on the log book at the nurses station.

If you have not returned to your room by the time you have given us, we will initiate our “Code Purple” to locate you, to assure your safety. “Code Purple” alerts our staff to search for missing patients.

PWN oo

We thank you for choosing Baptist Health Corbin for your health care needs.

I have read and understand the above guidelines are for my safety during my hospitalization at Baptist Health Corbin.

Physicians signature

BLOOD TRANSEUSION AND BLOOD PRODUCTS RISKS, BENEFITS, & ALTERNATIVES-If you wish not to receive blood or blood products please request to sign a refusal form, 1) Some surgical procedures may require blood

transfusions or blood products. My doctor has informed me that I may need to have blood products administered during the course of my treatment. 2) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products invotves the introduction of the product into onc of my veins, commonly in the arm using a needle. I understand that the amount of blood products administered is determined by my doctor based upon my particular needs. 3) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products may expose me to certain risks. Minor or temporary reactions, such as slight bruising, swelling, or local infection of the area where the needle pierces the skin is not uncommon. Non-serious reactions may include headache, fever, ora mild skin reaction, such as itching or a rash. Rare, but serious reactions are possible such as the transmission of diseases including hepatitis and AIDS, which require lengthy treatment and can be fatal. It has been further explained that while procedures for screening blood products for diseases such as AIDS and hepatitis are effective, these procedures are not perfect and there is a risk of expasure to AIDS, hepatitis, av other bload-borne disease. I have taken all of these sks into consideration and have decided that the benefits of receiving blood products outweigh the risks. 4) I have been made aware of the reasonably available alternatives to the administration of blood products and the risks and benefits of those alternatives including autologous blood transfusion, directed donation and not having any blood products administered. I understand that I may be able to donate one of several units of my own blood to be used during planned elective surgery and that my doctor must determine my ability to donate for myself. This is called autologous transfusion,

If I choose to have an autologous transfusion, I understand that the last unit of blood must be donated one week prior to expected use. I also understand that ] may choose a directed donation. A directed donation means I am allowed to select

ANESTHESIA-I understand that this procedure may require anesthesia. An anesthesiologist has or will explain the anesthesia alternatives. Complications from any type of anesthetic are infrequent, but do occur, Complications are more frequent for patients in poor health than for patients in good health. Complications associated with all types of anesthesia include temporary or permanent damage to teeth, lips, nose, throat, voice box, windpipe, eyes, nerves, or blood vessels. Drug reaction may occur and may be minor cr serious even requiring resuscitation. Breathing problems may develop.

Headache, back pain, some degree of awareness, infection, nausea and/or vomiting may occur. There are some very serious complications, which are uncommon, but can occur. These include paralysis, brain damage, or death. There are other potential complications depending on your situation; however, it is not possible to advise you of every imaginable complication. The purpase of this is not to frighten you, but to properly inform you. Serious complications are unlikely. The benefits of surgery and anesthesia, except in extreme circumstances, outweigh the potential risks. I agree that the potential benefit of the procedure(s) outweighs the potential risks of anesthesia.

I understand that my procedure is scheduled to be done with one of the following:

O General Anesthesia-monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist with or without a CRNA O Monitored Anesthesia Care-possible sedation, monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist or a CRNA, ps? fE Regional Anesthesia-monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist or a CRNA (L- yak 7% =

Ol Local Anesthesia-care managed by the Physician and monitored by a registered nurse

O Moderate Sedation-care managed by the Physician and monitored by a registered nurse

I understand that [ must be:

1.) Accompanied by a responsible adult.

2.) Nat take public transportation without the presence of another adult.

3.) Not drive a car, ingest alcoholic beverages, or depressant medication without direct permission from the physician, for a minimum of 24 hours.

4.) Not make any important decisions or sign any important documents or checks for 24 to 36 hours

‘There is the possibility that any of the services provided abave may convert to a general anesthetic. These same complications may occur with local anesthesia. It may become necessary for the anesthesiologist to render care in a life-threatening emergency situation without my previous consent. BAPTIST HEALTH Visit##: 70000259923 [DOB-3822] TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT & SMOKING CESSATION = 08: 12/28/1883 Sly F = MRN: 728945 {EID IDEN GNI OE) AON 0 UU MED A a

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobaceo Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products inctuding cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health’s campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, If needed. | understand that $ am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that § will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most pecple. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me? « Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer.

* Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * $moking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. * $moking increases acid in your stornach, which leads to ulcers Why is quitting gqaod for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit? First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are avalilabie. Talk to ex-smokers about what hetped them quit.

Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are wilting to help you quit or whe will quit smoking with you.

Before your gult date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention te when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at feast at first when you are quitting. Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor.

Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

For Additional Help, please contact the Kentucky Quit Line: 1-800-Quit Now (1-[PHONE-3823]} or TDY/TDD 1-[PHONE-3824]

Activity: Return to your usual activities

Other Instructions: + Drink plenty of fluids * Lie on your sides, * Keep bladder empty. - Do not smoke. if you smoke, please try nat to smoke for at least 2 hours before coming for your next test. Smoking changes the test results.

Movement of the baby: + Anytime you think your baby isn't moving as much as it should: you need to count the babies movements in the following manner:

Choose a 1 hour time period, during the time your baby Is most active. Lay down on your side, Count each time the baby kicks, rolls over or moves in any way. The baby should move at least 6 times, in a hour.If the baby does not move six times in one hour,then come to the hospital. Go to fabor hall and let the nurse know that your baby is not moving as it should.

PRENATAL MULTI-VITAMIN.

Take this medicine by mouth in the following dose: 1 multi-vitamin tablet once a day.

Vitamins are used when the dally diet may not have enough to maintain good health. When you are pregnant, your body needs more vilamins. This vitamin is made especially for use during pregnancy. A side effect may Include an upset stamach,

Follow these instructions:

* Take this vitamin with food to avoid an upset stomach.

* Deo not stop taking this vitamin wilhout talking with your doctor,

* Store this medicine away from heat, moisture or direct lighl.

« Ifyou miss a dose, skip the missed dose and go back to your regular schedule, Do not double the doses,

Call your doctor if you have: * a conlinuous upset stomach. ¢ any new or severe symptoms,

VITAMIN B COMPLEX Take this medicine by mouth in the following dose: 1 TAB DAILY

Vitamin B complex includes the following vitamins: thiamine (Vitamin B1), riboflavin (Vitamin B2), niacin (Vitamin 63), pantothenic acid (Vitamin B5), pyridoxine (Vitamin B6), biotm, folic acid, and vitamin G12. Each vitamin performs its own unique function in your body. This supplement may be used in managing diets of people with or at risk for cardiovascular disease, peripheral vascutardisease and cerebrovascular disease. This supplement may be used for other reasons as well, as prescribed by your doctor.

Side effects may inciude:

- mild diarrhea

+ Itching

* numbness or tingling in your arms or legs

- tiredness

Other side effects may occur, but are not as common,

Follow these instructions while using this medicine: = Eat a balanced diet. Foods high in vitamin B include: * eggs * oatmeal » whole grains * peas, beans, other vegetables * fish (especially salmon and tuna) and meat + liver * soy * bananas * peanuts + potatoes * Store this supplement away from heat, moisture, or direct light.

VITAMIN D

Take the vitamin D supplement by mouth in the following dose: 1 TAB DAILY

Vitamin Dis a vitamin that your body neads. Ills found in food and can be made in your body after exposure to uitraviolet light fram the sun, Vitamin D helps your body absorb calcium and phosphorus from your intestines, keeping a normal level of calejum and phosphorus In your bleed, Without enough vitamin D, you may develop astecporosis and your bones can become weak, thin and brittle. People with kidney, intestinal, or liver problems benefit from a supplement of vitamin O. This supplement may be used for other reasons, as prescribed by your healthcare provider.

Side effects occur If you take {oo much vitamin D. This may cause irregular heartbeats, nausea or vomiting, decreased appetile, weakness, and kidney stones.

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco?

* Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disaase.

+» Smoking/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends.

What are the Benefits of Quitting?

+ Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours,

* You save money and your taste and smell will improve.

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Hospital. Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be indepandent or may be employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers, Charges for such services are established by these providers.

3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: | authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures include, but are not limited to, providing information to: {a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMQs, insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or ite review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services provided to me on an Inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided far my transport to or from the Hospital; (b} any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, and physicians; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to ebtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; and (e) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital's Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, _ and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a "Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health's campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas}. | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicatine replacement products, if needed, I understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician. SMOKING CESSATION Wellness and Health Promotion; for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and falled. Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine, Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you fee! strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficutt. Why is smoking bad for me?

* Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer.

« Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs.

+ Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body.

* §moking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The ionger you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and prablems.

What Is the best way to quit?

* First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit. ¢ Seta specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date. * Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. * Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit. ¢ Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. . Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or wha. will quit smoking with you. + Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places. * Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking. - Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. . Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking. « Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes. . Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor. * Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date. | have reqeived the aboye information about Baptist Health’s Tobacco Free Environment and smoking cessation. Signatur Date:

For Additionat’ Help, please contact the Kentucky Quit Line: 4-800-Quit Now (1-[PHONE-3892]) or TDY/TDD 1-[PHONE-3893]

DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

Thank you for choosing Baptist Health! You can view your lab results (36 hours afler discharge), discharge instructions, a list of procedures performed and

other details of your visil online using our secure website, My Baptist Health KY¥.com. Check your email for your personal invitation, and click on the My

The following information will help you return to your best tevel of health. Everyone's recovery Is different.

Normal Discharge Home This Infarmation Is About Your Follow Up Care

Call Travis Gilbert, MD if you have any problems or concerns, You can reach Travis Gilbert al [PHONE-3992], + Trillium Way Suite 200, Corbin, KY, 40701.

This Information Is About Your tliness and Diagnosis

ANTENATAL:

Today you have come to the hospital for observation of labor. The results of this visl seem notmal and you may go home.

Do the following:

Aciivity: Return to your usual activities

Other Instructions: - Drink plenty of fluids + Lie on your sides. « Keep bladder ernpty. » Bo not smoke. If you smoke, ptease try not to smoke for at least 2 hours before coming for your next test. Smoking changes the test results.

Movement of the baby: + Anytime you think your baby isn’t moving as much as it should: you need to count the babies movements In the following manner:

Choose a 1 hour time period, during the time your baby is most active. Lay down on your side. Count each time the baby kicks, rolls over or moves In any way, The baby should move at least 6 times, in a hour.If the baby does not move six times in one hour, then come to the hospital. Ga to labor hall and let lhe nurse know that your baby is net moving as it should.

Return to the hospital If you have any of the foflowing:

* Water leaks or breaks.

+ Vaginal bleeding more than a period or any bright red bleeding. Contractions that progressively get stronger, last longer- and get closer

together that are not relleved by walking or resting on your sides.

* Experience severe, sharp, or rigid abdomen (belly) pain that is continuous.

Call your Doctors if you have:

This Is Information About Your New Medications - Start taking as prescribed.

PRENATAL MULTIVITAMIN,

Take this medicine by mouth in the following dose: 1 multi-vitamin tabletonce a day,

Vitamins are used when the daily diel may not have enough to maintain good health. When you are pregnant, your body needs more vitamins. This vitamin is made especially for use during pregnancy. A side effect may include an upset stomach,

Follow these Instructions:

Take this vitamin with food to avoid an upset stomach.

Do not stop taking this vitamin without talking with your doctor,

Store this medicine away from heat, moisture or direct fight.

If you miss a dose, skip the missed dose and go back to your regular schedule, Do not double the doses.

«

eae

Call your doctor if you have; * acantinugus upset stomach, * any new or severe symptoms.

VITAMIN B COMPLEX Take this medicine by mouth in the following dose: 1 tab daily

Vitamin 8 complex includes the following vilamins: thiamine (Vitamin B1}, riboflavin (Vitamin B2), niacin (Vitamin B3), pantothenic acid (Vitamin BS), pyridoxine (Vitamin B6), biotin, folic acid, and vitamin 812, Each vitamin performs its own unique function in your body. This supplement may be used in managing diets of people with or at risk for cardiovascular disease, peripheral vascular disease and cerebrovascular disease. This supplement may be used for other reasons as well, as preserlbed by your doctor.

Slde effects may include:

mild diarrhea

« itching

* numbness or tingling in your arms or legs

* tiredness

Other side effecis may occur, bul are nol as common.

Follow these instructions while using this medicine: * Eat a balanced diet. Foods high in vitamin B include:

*" eggs

oatmeal

+ whole grains

“ peas, beans, other vegetables

+ fish (especially salmon and tuna) and meat

- liver

- soy

- bananas

* peanuts

* potatoes * Store this supplement away from heat, moisture, or direct light.

VITAMIN D Take the vilamin D supptement by mouth in the following dose: 1 tab daily ene ae RRS, Sh aaa MOAacaciaTie nen haa eietiehai_ Miechtnchehen arab eae a

Vitamin Dis a vitamin that your body needs. tt is found In food and can be made in your body after exposure to ultraviolet light from the sun. Vitamin D helps your body absorb calcium and phosphorus from your intestines, keeping a normai levet of calcium and phosphorus In your blood, Without enough vitamin D, you may develop osteoporosis and your bones can become weak, thin and brittle. People with kidney, intestinal, or liver problems benefit from a supplement of vitamin D. This supplement may be used for other reasons, as Prescribed by your healthcare provider.

Side effects occur If you take too much vitamin D. This may cause irregular heartbeats, nausea or vomiting, decreased appetile, weakness, and kidney stones.

Please follow these instructions: + Do not take more of this supplement than prescribed. + Take this medicine with a full glass of water. + Eata balanced diet. Feods that are high in vitamin D include: * salmon + mackerel - cod liver oil * margarine » vitamin-P fortified mitk * vitamin-D fortified cereals « gysters and fish » Store this vitamin away from heat, moisture or direct tight. + H you miss a dose, skip the missed dose and go back to your regular schedule. Do not double the dosas.

Cail your healthcare provider if you have:

* any sign of allergy.

* an irregular heartbeat (feels fike your heart is "skipping beats" or beating very fast).

» unusual weakness, nausea or vomiting.

* any bothersome side effects.

Why should you step smoking or using Tobacco?

+ Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease.

+ Smoking/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends.

What are the Benefits of Quitting?

» Your chances of getting cancer decrease.

«Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours.

«You save money and your taste and smell will improve.

How to quit?

Healthy Heart Tips

- Eat more grains, starches, fruits and vegetables

+ Add as litile fat as possible (oil, margarine, mayonnaise, salad dressing, gtavies} to your food

« Avoid fried foods

« Select only lean meats, fish, poultry, in "deck of card” size portions

* Select only low-fat cheese

* Use skim or 1% milk and dalry products

* Limit your daily sodium intake in your diet

* Monitor weight daily on the same scale

* Develop a regular exercise regimen as tolerated under your physician's guidelines

» Take all prescribed medications as directed by yaur physician

* See your physician regularly for routine check-ups and contact them if any significant concerns develop

* If you have been diagnosed wiih Heart Failure and notice swelling of ankles/feet or have a two pound waight gain overnight, contact your physician.

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Haspital. Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assisiants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by these providers.

BAPTIST HEALTH Visit: 70000257038 [DOB-4012] TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT & SMOKING CESSATION = 908: 12/28/1083 Sty =F MRN: 728045 {EOI UA 0A) EA iW COO UA OED J

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supparts wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health’s campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn’t there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why Is smoking bad for me? *- Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer.

° Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. : . Smoking raises your bleod pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. . Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers

Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down.

The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit?

First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk te your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Join a support group for people whe are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you.

Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make 8 list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you wil do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor.

Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

DICTATING: Travis Gilbert, DO PRIMARY CARE PROVIDER:

DATE OF SURGERY: [DOB-4032]

DATE OF BIRTH: [DOB-6556]

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Dasires permanent sterilization. POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Desires permanent sterilization,

PROCEDURE PERFORMED: Laparoscopic tuhal ligation on the right side (the patient is status post left salpingectomy).

SURGEON: Travis Gilbert, DO. ANESTHESIA: General. COMPLICATIONS: None.

ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS: Minimal.

PROCEDURE: The patient was taken to the operating room, given genaral anesthesia, prepped and draped in the dorsal lithotomy position using Allen stirrups. The patient was sterilely prepared and draped. Manipulator was placed into the uterus. The surgeon was regloved and attention was made to the abdomen. We made a 5 mm incision into the umbilicus, inserted an Optiview trocar into the abdomen under direct visualization using the laparoscope. We filled the abdomen with Co2 gas up to 15 mmHg. We placed an 8 mm trocar suprapubically in the midline. We identified the right fallopian tube. The left fallopian tuba was absent and the ends seem very sealed up. We placed some Marcaine over the fallopian tube, placed a Filshie clip in the midportion of the tube until good blanching was noted. Removed thé C02 gas, removed the trocars, closed the skin incisions with 4-0 Monocryl, and placed surgical adhesive over the incision. Theres ware no complications during the procedure. All the counts were correct.

SEDATION DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

Thank you for choasing Baptist Health! You can view your lab results (36 hours after discharge), discharge instructions, a list of procedures performed and other details of your visit ontine using our secure website, My Baptist Health KY¥.com. IMPORTANT: The following information wil help you return to your best level of --heaith> Everyone's recovery is‘different-- ~—~ — —T me

GENERAL ANESTHESIA.

You have had a procedure that required general anesthesia. You were given a

medicine to help you go to steap and not feel pain.

Follow these Instructions:

* Po not drive a car or operate machinery for 24 hours.

* Have a RESPONSIBLE PERSON stay wth you today for your protection and safety.

* Go directly horne and rest quietly today.

* You may be up to the bathroom and to the table for meals with assistance. Inthe morning you do not need anyone with you if you are not dizzy or having any problems.

* ODonot make any Important decisions or sign any legal documents in the next 24 hours.

Diet:

* Begin with liquids and add light foods if nct nauseated (jello, soup, juice, toast).

s Avoid spicy, greasy, or gas-producing foods (these foods may cause nausea).

¢ Donet drink any alcoholic beverages including beer for 24 hours.

Call your doctor if you have:

* any blue or gray skin color.

* continuous vomiting.

* trouble breathing.

* any new problems or concerns.

This Information Is About Your Follow Up Care

An appointment has been made for you an JULY 8TH AT 11:40 AM to see Travis Gilbert, MD. You can reach Dr. Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-5040], 1 Tollium Way Suite 200, Corbin, KY, 40701.

This information ts About Procedures

TUBAL LIGATION (LAPAROSCOPY),

Atubal ligation is a permanent form of birth cantrot. Both of your fallopian tubes were clipped, cut, or sealed shut, This prevents the release of an egg from your ovary to your uterus. It also prevents sperm from being able ta fertilize the egg. The dactor made a small cut near your belly button and one in the lower part of your abdomen, You may have some pain and bruising around the incisions, Carbon dioxide was inserted into your abdomen to inflate your abdomen. Your body will simply absorb the carbon dioxide. You may feel pain in your shoulder for the next few days. This is a typical response when your breathing muscle

HOM

70000267438

{diaphragm} is imitated by carbon dioxide, You may also feel the need te urinate more often due to the carbon dioxide putting pressure on your bladder.

You may take a shower.. Keep your bandage dry.

At home, please follow these instructions:

* Take your pain medicines exactly as prescribed.

* Slowly Increase your activity.

* To avold trouble with constipation: * Drink atleast 8 glasses of water or Juice each day unless your doctor

—has‘Umlited- your fluldss— to ¢ Eathigh flbar foods (whole grain breads and cereals, vegetables and

fruits), * Keep all follow-up appointments with your doctor,

Call your doctor if you have:

+ increased bleeding or drainage from your incision. swelling, redness, or opening of your Incision, foul smell from your incision or bandage.

red streaks from your incision.

chlits or fever over 101 degrees F (by mouth). pain not helped by your pain medicine, trouble or paln with breathing.

* nausea or vomiting.

* trouble passing urine.

* increased or foul smelling vagina! discharge. * any new problems or concems.

This is Information About Your New Modications - Start taking as prescribed.

eae ee

«

IBUPROFEN (Motrin, Advil, Rufen, Nuprin)

Take this medicine with food and an eight-ounce glass of water in the following dose: 800 mg by mouth every 6 hours if needed.

WARNING: Do not take this medicine If you are pregnant.

This medicine is used to reduce pain and inflammation (redness and swelling). {tls also used to treat arthritis, muscle aches, headaches, athletic injuries, gout and menstrual cramps. Ibuprofen also reduces a fever. Thls medicine may be used for other reasons, as prescribed by your doctor.

Side offacts may include:

* an upset stomach or abdominal palin

¢ heartburm

* dizziness or drowsiness

*« abdominal or rectal bleeding

Other side effects may occur, but are not as common. Allergy would shaw up as! rash or itching, facial or throat swelling, wheezing or shortness of breath.

Follow these Instructions:

» Donottake any other pain or antiinflammatory medicines (such as aspirin, naproxen, ketoprofen, celecoxib, others) while taking this medicine. (Read the fabels on all non-prescription pain and fever-reducing medicines.)

« Store this medicine away from heat, moisture or direct light.
