HYDROCODONE AND ACETAMINOPHEN (Vicodin, Vicodin ES, Lortab, Lortab elixir, Zamicet, Norcoe, Zydone, Anexsia, Anolor, Bancup HC)

Take this medicine by mouth with food in the following dose: one Smg/a25mg tablet every 4 to 6 hours if needed for paln. Do not take more than 8 tablets per day (24 hours).

This js a mixture of medicines (hydrocodone and acetamtnophen) used to relieve moderate to severe pain. This medicine may be used for other reasons, as prescribed by your doctor.

Side effacts may Include:

* sleepiness or dizziness

* upset stomach, nausea, or vomiting

* constipation

Other side effects may occur, but are not as common. Allergy would show up as: rash or itching, facial or throat swelling, wheezing or shortness of breath. This medicine can be habit forming if used for a lang pericd of time.

At home, please follow these instructions: + Never take more of this medicine than prescribed. Too much acetaminophen in your body can cause liver damage. » Read the labels of non-prescription medicines before taking them. Many contain acetaminophen. To avoid an overdose, do not take any other medicines that contain acetaminophen. This medicine may make you dizzy or drowsy, There is a higher chance you could fall. To prevent falling: , + Stand up slowly. If you feel dizzy, hold on to something sturdy until the fealing goes away. + Do not do any activities that require alertness until you know you can safely do se. » Use plenty of light in your house. * Reduce clutter in your home. Do not take sleeping medicines or sedatives while being treated with this medicine.

* “Watth for signs of dependence: * feeling that you "cannot live withoul this medicine" * you need more of this medicine than before to get the same relief

* Do not drink alcohol, drive, or operate machinery until you know how this medicine affects you.

* Store this medicine away from heat, moisture, or direct tight.

« {fyou are taking this on a regular schedule and you miss a dose, take It as soon as possible. Ifitis almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose and return to your regular schedule. Do not double the doses.

* Talk with your doctor before taking any other medicines (including vitamins and herbals) as you may require additional monitoring.

Centact your health care provider Immediately if you have: * any sign of dependence or allergy.

> increased pain not helped by the pain medicine.

* slow, weak breathing.

* selzures.

* slow or Irregular heart beat.

+ a yellow-color to your skin or eyes, or dark urine.

* severe stomach pain.

* unusual or extreme tiredness.

Contact your health care provider as soon as possible if you have any new or bothersome symptoms.

General Health Information

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco?

» Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease.

¢ Smoking/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends,

What are the Bonpfits of Quitting?

* Your chances of getting cancer decrease.

* Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours.

« You save money and your taste and small will Improve.

How to quit?

» Set a quit date and stick to it-not a single puff more.

: Be sure to have a strong support group such as: family, friends, co-worker, and your doctor.

* Eat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself from smoking.

Need Help?

Cali Kentucky's Tobacco Quitline: 1-[PHONE-5043] (1-800-Quit-Now) or your local Health Department.

Whitley County Health Dept: [PHONE-5044] (Williamsburg) [PHONE-5045] (Corbin)

YOU ARE GOING TO HAVE A PROCEDURE (Pre-Proc Jeashinal

Thank you for choosing our hospital for your procedure. We will work together with you and your family to insure this Js a positive experience. You are a very imponant part of your recovery. Ask questions if you have any concerns.

‘This Information Is About Procedures

Scheduled Procedure:

laparoscopic tubal fallope filshie clipping Arrival Date and Time;

06/18/15 at 9:30

Preparation Instructions:

Shower the morning of surgery with an antibacterial soap such as Cial, Follow the chlorhexedine cloth skin prap instructions sheet provided by the Preadmission Nurse.

Medication Instructions:

On the morning of surgery take your regularty scheduled medications with just a slp of water. You may also take your pain medications. The only exception to this is OO NOT TAKE ANY DIABETES MEDICATIONS. This includes all types of insulin and any pills that you take to control your bload suger,

Medications you need to take with just a SIP of WATER the morning of your procedure:

General instructions:

Do not eat or drink anything after midnight the night prior to your surgery. This includes water, mints, gum and tobacco spit. You may brush your teeth. BO NOT SWALLOW ANYTHING UNLESS INSTRUCTED TO TAKE YOUR MORNING MEDICATIONS. Do not smoke ar drink alcohal 24 hours before surgery. Bring all your papers given te you by your doctors office including X-rays, consent forms, clearance forms or reports.

If you receive anesthesia or sedation, a responsible adult mus accompany you upon discharge and remain with you for 24 hours following your procedure.

Wear comfortable clothing. Do not wear contacts or heavy makeup. Leave jewelry and vatuables at home. Bring a case for your glasses. Bring your crutches, walker of other assistive device if applicable.

You will change into a hospital gown, Your temperature, heart rate and blood Pressure will be checked. Adults and older children will have an IV started and you may receive medication through your IV to help you relax and/or Prepare for your procedure.

then you will be diracted to the surgery department check-in window. Leave your papenwork with the staff and have a seat in the surgery walting area. You will be called into pre-op by your nurse at the appropriate time.

RISKS, BENEFITS, & ALTERNATIVES. The physician has explained to me: (1) the nature of the proposed procedure; (2) the potential risks, benefits and side effects, including potential problems that might occur during recuperation; (3) the likelihood of achieving goals; (4) reasonable alternatives to the procedure, if any; (5) the relevant benefits and risks, and side effects associated with those a}terngtiyes, including the possible results of not receiving care, treatment, and services, likelihood of achieving goals an y pt receiving care, treatment and services.

BLOOD TRANSFUSION AND BLOOD PRODUCTS RISKS, BENEFITS, & ALTERNATIVES-If you wish not to receive blood or blood products please request to sign a refusal form. 1) Some surgical procedures may require blood transfusions or blood products. My doctor has informed me that I may need to have blood products administered during the course of my treatment. 2) It has been explained 10 me that the administration of blood products invalves the introduction of the product into one of my veins, commonly in the arm using a needle. [ understand that the amount of blood products administered is determined by my doctor based upon my particular needs. 3) It has been explained to me thal the administration of blood products may expose me to certain risks. Minor or temporary reactions, such as slight bruising, swelling, or local infection of the area where the needle pierces the skin is not uncommon. Non-serious reactions may include headache, fever. or a mild skin reaction, such as itching or arash. Rare, but serious reactions are possible such as the transmission of diseases including hepatitis and AIDS, which require lengthy treatment and can be fatal. It has been further explained that while procedures for screening blood products for diseases such as AIDS and hepatitis are effective, these procedures are not perfect and there is a risk of exposure to AIDS, hepatitis, or other blood-borne disease. 1 have taken all of these risks into consideration and have decided that the benefits of receiving blood products outweigh the risks. 4) I have been made aware of the reasonably available alternatives to the administration of blood products and the risks and benefits of those alternatives including autologous blood transfusion, directed donation and not having any blood products administered. I understand that I may be able ta donate one of several units of my own blood to be used during planned elective surgery and that my doctor must determine my ability to donate for myself. This is called autologous transfusion.

If | choose to have an autologous transfusion, 1 understand that the last unit of blood must be donated one week prior to expected use. [also understand that I may choose a directed donation. A directed donation means 1 am allowed to select

my own donors. There is no scientific evidence that directed donations are safer but I understand many patients feel more comfortable designating their own donors. The designated donor can donate within thirty (30) days of the anticipated need for the blood. It takes at least seventy-two (72) hours for a unit from a directed donor to be processed and available for transfusion. These arrangements must be made by my doctor,

TISSUE REMOVAL-I understand that this procedure{s) may result in the removal of body tissue or body parts. I consent to their disposal according to the hospital policies and the recommendations of my physician. SALES REPRESENTATIVES-1 understand that there may be a representative from a company that furnishes equipment to surgeons and hospitals present in the room duting my surgical procedure. I also understand that his/her function is strictly to observe and advise, and that this person has no part in my direct care.

ANESTHESIA -I understand that this procedure may require anesthesia, An anesthesiologist has or will explain the anesthesia alternatives. Complications from any type of anesthetic are infrequent, but do occur. Complications are more frequent for patients in poor health than for patients in good health. Complications associated with all types of anesthesia include temporary or permanent damage to teeth, lips, nose, throat, voice box, windpipe, eyes, nerves, or blood vessels. Drug reaction may occur and may be minor or serious even requiting resuscitation. Breathing problems may develop.

Headache, back pain, some degree of awareness, infection, nausea and/or vomiting may occur. There are some very serious complications, which are uncommon, but can occur. These include paralysis, brain damage, or death. There are other potential complications depending on your situation, however, it is not passible to advise you of every imaginable complication. The purpose of this is not to frighten you, but ta properly inform you. Serious complications are unlikely. The benefits of surgery and anesthesia, except in extreme circumstances, outweigh the potential risks. I agree that the potential benefit of the procedure(s) outweighs the potential risks of anesthesia.

T understand that my procedure is scheduled to be done with one of the following:

General Anesthesia~monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist with or without a CRN

Monitored Anesthesia Care-possibte sedation, monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist or a CRNA O Regional Anesthesia-monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist ar a CRNA O Loeal Anesthesia-caye managed by the Physician and monitored by a registered nurse CO) Moderate Sedation-care managed by the Physician and monitored by a registered aurse

I understand that I must be:

1.) Accompanied by a responsible adult.

2.) Not take public transportation without the presence of another adult,

3.) Not drive 4 car, ingest alcoholic beverages, or depressant medication without direct permission from the physician, for a minimum of 24 hours.

4.) Not make any important decisions or sign any important documents or checks for 24 to 36 hours

There is the possibility that any of the services provided above may convert to a general anesthetic. These same complications may occur with local anesthesia. It may become necessary for the anesthesiologist to render care in a life-threatening emergency situation without my previous consent. If during a local anesthetic an emergent situation arises I understand that my physician may consult with an anesthesiologist to render the necessary emergency medical care.

DNR STATUS WILL BE SUSPENDED DURING SURGICAL PROCEDURE.

Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by these providers,

Examples of such uses and disclosures include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital; (b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, and physicians; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; and (e) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital's Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | iso consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health’s campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that |! am to remain in my treatment area at alll times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you fee! strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me? * Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer. + Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your biood to circulate throughout your body. * Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers

Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit?

First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to heip you quit or who will quit smoking with you.

Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor,

Throw away al! cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

ee @ © @ © © © © © © & @

aptist Health's Tobacco Free Environment and smoking cessation. Signat¢fe: / _ 4 Date: Lp ~{6 79

For Additional Help, please contact the Kentucky Quit Line: 1-800-Quit Now (1-[PHONE-5062]) or TDY/TDD 1-[PHONE-5063]

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health's campus or at any facility owned, teased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that! will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicoline replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facitity, that ! will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

“—~-— SMOKING.CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers; those who-are-around_smok rs (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good, Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This Is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body Is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you feet strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me? * Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer. - Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. * Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers

Why is quitting good for me? As s00n as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit?

First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date,

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Jain a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you,

Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you wil save by not buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor,

Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn’t there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me? * Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer. e Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * Smoking taises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. e Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit?

° First,. decide in your mind that you are going to quit. + - Set-a specific.dateto stop smoking and tell other people that youare-goingte sioa- smoking on yeur.quit date. oo ted * Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are avajlable. * Talk to ex-smokers about what hetped them quit. * Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times . Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you. + Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places. . Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking. * Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. . Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking. * Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes. ° Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor. *« Throw away ali cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date. | have received the above information about Baptist Health's Tobacco Free Iknvironment and smoking cessation. 2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

in most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Hospital. Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers, Charges for such services are established by these providers.

MOEA ER A

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tebacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tabacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health’s campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. f further understand that If 1 leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Weliness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and falled.

Smoking cessation is quitting smeking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most peopfe. The chemical, nicotine, which you Inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine Is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn’t there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me? * Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer. * §moking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. . Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers

Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit?

First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you.

Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor.

Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

2, PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Hospital, Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, cerlified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be employed by other health care organizations. You may recelve a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by these providers.

3. CONSENT TO USE ANDO DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION:

| authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use and disclose information about me to carry oul treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disclosures include, but are not limited ta, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare anc Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services pravided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital; (o) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, and physicians; (d} any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; and (e) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent inchides the release of medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcaholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device In accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

8aptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Eree” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco, These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health’s campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Exctuding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. I further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that t will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician,

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers {second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking Is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemicai, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine, Dopamine is @ chemical that

Why is smoking bad for me?

° Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer, * Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body. * Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down. The longer yau do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit?

. First, decide in your mind that yOU are going to quit.

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date,

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Joln a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you.

Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes,

Begin an exercise program Suggested by your doctor.

Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHEA HEALTH CAE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees or agents of the Hospital. Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent or may be emptoyed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by these providers. ’

\ is smoking bad for me? * Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer. BAPTIST HEALTH Visit#: 70000356633 [DOB-5172] TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT & SMOKING CESSATION = 908: [DOB-5173] Sty =F MRN: 726045 {IOAN UR CD END EO UE

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health’s campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need-to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may cornplicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION - ° Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed. -:

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult,

Why is smoking bad for me? +” - Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer.

® Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. ° Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blocd to circulate throughout your body. -*- Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers

Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to go down.

The longer you do net smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the best way to quit? © - one First, decide in your mind that you are going ‘to quit. ‘

Set a specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date. Talk to your doctor about medications that canbe used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit. Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit of whe will quit smoking withs you. Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places: + ‘ Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smake. Notice what things make you feel like smoking. Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. - Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking. . Think of what you will da with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes. Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor. .. Throw away. all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

Normal Discharge Home This Information Is About Your Follow Up Care

An appointment has been made for you on KEEP REGULAR APPT to sea Travis Gilbert,MD. You can reach Dr, Travis Gilbert at [PHONE-5305], 1 Trillium Way Suite 200, Corbin, KY, 40701,

This information ts About Procedures

NON-STRESS TEST

Anon-stress test is done to measure how your baby's heart rate responds to its own movements. Healthy babies’ heart rates increase when they move, then decrease when they rest. This Is called a “reactive stress test". When a babyis moving around, adequate blood supply and oxygen is needed for the baby's heart rate to increase. When the oxygen levels are low, a baby's heart rate may F101 respond normally ta movement. Low oxygen levels can often be caused by problems with the placenta or umbilical cord.

We will be place a strap around your abdomen that holds two monitors. One monitor will check for and measure contractions of your uterus. The other monitor wilt measure your baby's heartbeat. This test is meant te be done when your baby is not under any stress. The test usually takes 20 to 30 minutes.

ifthe baby’s heart rate does notincrease at feast twice with movementwithin 20 minutes, we cailit a "non-reactive non-stress test". This does nol mean thereis @ problem. Further testing may be needed.

You may return to your usual activities as direcled by your healthcare provider.

Contact your heaithcare provider If:

* your water breaks.

* you have bleeding fram your vagina that ls more than spotting. * you have constant, severe abdomina! pain.

* you have strong, regular contractions § minutes apart or less. * you have any new or unusual symptoms,

This Information ts About Your Iltness and Diagnosis

PREMATURE LABOR (Early Labor)

The due date for your pregnancy is [DOB-5306].

Regular contractions of your uterus more than a few weeks before your due date is called "premature labor". Many things can cause early labor. We often do not knew the exact cause. Early tabor can be risky for your baby. You must do all that you can to avold it, and treat it early.

Follow these instructions:

IAI AA

70000245717

+ Rest in bed and lie on your taft side. This Increases the blood flow to your uterus,

+ Do not stand for long periods of time.

« De not lift heavy things, do heavy work, play active sports, or climb stairs frequently.

+ Go not ge on tong car trips.

* Drink extra liquids throughout the entire day (at least 10 large glasses of water or Juice), Avoid caffeine, alcohol, tobacco, coffee, tea and colas.

* Watch for the following symptoms of premature labor:

: increasing contractions (tight feeling in your belly or petvis) * blood or other fluid from your vagina

* backache

* flow pelvic pressure

* Do not "prepare your nipples” for breast feeding. Do not allow another child to breast feed. This could start contractions.

Call your doctor if you have:

* chills or fever.

* any signs of premature labor IIstad above,

* burning when you pass your urine or frequent urination.

* vaginal discharge or a foul smell to your vaginal discharge. * any new problems or concarns.

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco?

* Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease.

» Smoking/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging process, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends,

What are the Benefits of Quitting?

« Your chances of getting cancer decrease.

» Your chance of heari attack decreases within 24 hours.

* You save money and your taste and smell will improve.

How to quit?

+ Set a quit date and stick to it-nol a single puff more.

+ Be sure to have a strong suppon group such as: family, friends, co-worker, and your doctor.

« Eat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself from smoking.

Need Help?

Call Kentucky's Tobacco Quitline: 1-[PHONE-5307] (1-800-Quit-Now) or your local Health Department.

Healthy Heart Tips

* Eat more grains, starches, fruits and vegetables

- Add as little fat as possible (oil, margarine, mayonnaise, salad dressing, graviss) to your food

- Avoid fried foods

- Select only 'ean meats, fish, pouliry, in “deck of card” size Portions

¢ Select only low-fat cheese

+ Use skim of 1% milk and dairy products

* Limit your dally sodium intake in your diel

* Monltor weight daily on the same scale

+ Develop a regular exercise regimen as tolerated under your physician's guidelines

* Take all prescribed medications as directed by your physician

* See your physician regularly for routine check-ups and contact them if any significant concerns develop

* Ifyou have been diagnosed with Heart Failure and notice swelling of ankles/feel or have a two pound weight gain overnight, contact your physician,

Stroke Prevention and Risk Factors - Overweight

- Sedentary Lifestyle

« Smoking

* High Blood Pressure

- Diabetes

- Poor Diet

« Heart Disease

- Family History

+ Carotid Artery Disease

* Increased Alcohol Consumption - Prior Stroke or TIA

Warning Signs of a Stroke Include but are not limited to: + Sudden numbness or weakness of arms or legs

+ Slurred speech or unable to understand speech

* Headache like you have never had before

+ Troubte seeing from one eye or both

+ Trouble walking, loss of coordination, or dizziness

= Sudden confusion

Call 911 immediately if you notice any of these warning signs.

BAPTIST HEALTH Visit#: 70000245717 [DOB-5318] TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT & SMOKING CESSATION —-908:[DOB-6556] 31y_F MRN: 728945 (ULI XI NOY LMM PEA A

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT

Baptist Health supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free”! environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to alt tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health's Campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist «Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). | understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed, | understand that f am to remain in my treatment area at all times, | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that } will not be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

SMOKING CESSATION

Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers (second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed.

Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale Into your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemica! that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you feel strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is sa difficult.

Why is smoking bad for me?

* Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer. * Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs. * Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder far your blood to circulate throughout your body. cd Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which teads to ulcers Why is quitting good tor me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases start to ge down. The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What Is the best way to quit?

First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit.

Set a specific date to step smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date.

Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available, Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit.

Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group participation raises your chance of success by eight times. Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you.

Before your quit date, only aliow yourself ta smoke in one or two places.

Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you fee! like smoking.

Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting, Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking.

Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes.

Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor,

Throw away ail cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date.

Corneal abrasion to the right eye. No foreign body.

INSTRUCTIONS

Warnings: GENERAL WARNINGS: Return or contact your physician immediately if your condition worsens or changes unexpectedly, if not improving as expected, or if other problems arise.

Prescription Medications: Tylenol with Codeine Tylenol #3 (30 mg / 300 mg) : take 1 tablet orally every 6 hours as needed for pain. Dispense fifteen (15).

Gentamicin ophthalmic ointment 0.3% : apply 0.5 inch to inner aspect of the lowsr lid on the affected eye avery 8 hours for 7 days. Dispense three and one half (3.5) grams. .

Follow-up with: Benjamin Mackey MD, Ophthalmology, [PHONE-5365], 281 N Col Trillium Way, , Corbin, 40701 Follow up tomorrow.

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION yrneal Abrasion

_ whe cornea is the clear part in the front of the eye. This sensitive area is very painful when injured. There may be tearing and your vision may be blurry

until healing occurs. You may be sensitive to Light.

This part of the body heals quickly. You can expect the pain to go away

Page 1 of 5

11 of 862




MILLS , AMY 70000376781 RHRKAS

within 24-48 hours, If the abrasion is large or deep, your doctor may apply an eye patch, although this is not always done. An antibiotic ointment or sys -drops may also be used to prevent infection. tumbing drops may be usad to relisve the pain temporarily so that your eyes ‘an be examined. However, these drops cannot be prescribed for home use because that would slow down the healing process. Also, if you can't feel your eye, there is a chance of accidentally injuring your eye further without knowing it, Home Care: A cold pack (ice in a plastic bag, wrapped in a towel) may be applied over the eye (or eyapatch) for 20 minutes at a time, to reduce pain. You may use acetaminophen (Tylenol) or ibuprofen (Motrin, Advil) to control pain, unless another pain medicine was prescribed. [NOTE: If you hava chronic liver or kidney disease or ever had a stomach ulcer or GI bleeding, talk with your doctor before using these medicines. ] Rest your eyes and do not read until symptoms are gone. If you use contact lenses, do not wear them until all symptoms are gone. If your vision is affected by the corneal abrasion or if an eyepatch was applied, DO NOT DRIVE a motor vehicle or operate machinery until all symptoms are gone. Otherwise, you would have trouble judging distances with only one eye. If your eyes are sensitive to light, try wearing sunglasses, or stay indoors, until symptoms go away. Follow Up as advised by our staff. Serious abrasions may be referred to an sye specialist (ophthalmologist). ff no patch was used but the pain continues for more than 48 hours, you should have another axam. Return to this facility or gontact the referral doctor to arrange this. If your eye was patched and if you were asked to remove the patch yourself, see your doctor or return to this facility if your pain is still present after the patch is removed. If you were given a return appointment for patch removal and re-exam, do not miss this. It could be harmful if the patch remains in place longer than advised. Get Prompt Medical Attention iff any of the following occur: Increasing eye pain or pain that does not improve after 24 hours Discharge from the eys Increasing redness of the eye or swelling of the eyelids Your vision gets worse Acetaminophen, Codeine Phosphate Oral tablet What is this medicine? : ACETAMINOPHEN; CODEINE (a set a MEE noe fen; KOR deen) is a pain reliever. It is used to treat mild to moderate pain. How should I use this medicine? Take this medicine by mouth with a full glass of water. Follow the directions on the prescription label, If the medicine upsets your stomach, take the medicine with food or milk. Do not take more medicine than you are told to take. alk to your pediatrician regarding the use of this medicine in children. yecial care may be needed. what side effects may I notice from receiving this medicine? Side effects that you should report to your doctor or health care professional as soon as possible: allergic reactions like skin rash, itching or hives, swelling of the face,

Page 2 of 5

12 of 862




MILLS , AMY 70000376781 *eKKAS

lips, or tongue

breathing difficulties, wheezing -confusion

‘ight headedness or fainting spells

evere stomach pain

yellowing of tha skin or the whites of the eyes

Side effects that usually do not require medical attention (report to your doctor or health care professional if they continus or are bothersome): dizziness

drowsiness

nausea, vomiting

What may interact with this medicine?

alcohol

antihistamines

benztropine

drugs for bladder problems like solifenacin, trospium, oxybutynin, tolterodine, hycosamina, and methscopolamine

drugs for breathing problems like ipratropium and tiotropium

drugs for certain stomach or intestine problems like propanthelins, homatropine methylbromide, glycopyrrolate, atropine, belladonna, and adicyclominea

medicines for depression, anxiety, or psychotic disturbances

medicines for sleap

muscle relaxants

naltrexons

narcotic medicines {opiates) for pain

phenothiazines liks parphenazine, thioridazina, chlorpromazine, mesoridazins, Eluphenazine, prochlorperazine, promazine, trifluoperazine

scopolamine

tramadol

trihexyphsnidyl

What if I miss a dosa?

If you miss a dose, take it as soon as you can. If it is almost time for your next dose, take only that dose. Do not take double or extra doses.

Where should I keep my medicine?

Keep out of the reach of children. This medicine can be abused. Keep your medicine in a safe place to protect it from theft. Do not share this medicine with anyone. Selling or giving away this medicine is dangerous and against the law.

Store at room temperature between 15 and 30 degrees ¢ (59 and 86 degrees F). Protect from light. Keep container tightly closed,

Throw away any unused medicine after the expiration'date. Discard unused medicine and used packaging carefully. Pets and children can bs harmed if they find used or lost packages.

What should I tell my health care provider hefore I take this medicine? They need to know if you have any of these conditions:

brain tumor

Crohn's diseases, inflammatory bowel disease, or ulcerative colitis

drink more than 3 alcohol containing drinks per day

vug abuse or addiction

jad injury

we8art or circulation problems

kidney disease or problems going to the bathroom

liver disease

Lung disease, asthma, or breathing problems

MILLS , AMY 70000376781 RREKAS

an unusual or allergic reaction to acetaminophen, codeine, salicylates, other opioid analgesics, other medicines, foods, dyes, or preservatives pregnant or trying to get pregnant

\weast~feeding

that should I watch for while using this medicine?

fell your doctor or health care professional if your pain does not go away, if it gets worse, or if you have new or a differant type of pain. You may develop tolerance to the medication. Tolerance means that you will need a higher doses of the medication for pain relis£. Tolerance is normal. and is expected if you take the medicine for a Long time.

Do not suddenly stop taking your medicine because you may develop a sevare reaction. Your body bacomes used to the medicine. This does NOT mean you are addicted. Addiction is a behavior related to gatting and using a drug for a non medical reason. If you have pain, you have a medical reason to take pain medicine. Your doctor will tell you how much medicine to take. If your doctor wants you to stop the madicine, the dose will be slowly lowered over time to avoid any side effects. .

You may get drowsy or dizzy. Do not drive, use machinery, or do anything that needs mental alertness until you know how this medicine affects you. Do not stand or sit up quickly, especially if you are an older patient. This reduces the risk of dizzy or fainting spells. Alcohol may interfere with the effect of this medicine. Avoid alcoholic drinks,

There are different types of narcotic medicines (opiates) for pain. If you take more than one type at the same time, you may have more side effects. Give your health care provider a list of all medicines you use. Your doctor will tell you how much madicine to take. Do not take more medicine than directed. Call emergency for help if you have problems breathing.

The medicine will cause constipation. Try to have a bowel movement at least every 2 to 3 days. If you do not have a bowel movement for 3 days, call your doctor cr health care professional.

Do not take Tylenol (acetaminophen) or medicines that have acetaminophen with this medicine. Too much acetaminophen can be very dangerous. Many nonprescription medicines contain acetaminophen. Always read the labels carefully to avoid taking more acetaminophen.

Immediately call your physician or get emergency help if you are breast-feeding and your baby is sleepier than usual, is limp, or has difficulty breastfeeding or breathing.

Why should you stop smoking or using Tobacco? Smoking/Tobacco use can cause Cancer, Heart and Lung Disease. * Smoking/Tobacco use is expensive, increases the aging procass, and second hand smoke hurts your family and friends. What are the Benefits of Quitting? ‘ Your chances of getting cancer decrease. Your chance of heart attack decreases within 24 hours. You save money and your taste and smell will improve. ow to quit? Set a quit date and stick to it-not a single puff more. Be sure to have a strong support group such as: family, frisnds, co-worker, and your doctor. ' Eat healthy, stay active and change your routine to distract yourself from smoking.

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL:

In most situations, the medical treatment provided to patients in the Hospital is given by physicians and other providers who are not employees ar agents of the Hospital. Such providers include, but are not limited to, anesthesiolagists, cardiologists, radiologists, emergency physicians, pathologists surgeons, certified registered nurse anesthetists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists. These providers may be independent ar may be employed by other health care organizations. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by these providers.

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT & SMOKING CESSATION i 2h Y ee wO84s FIO OME OTR) CO ELT AT A

TOBACCO FREE ENVIRONMENT Baptist Heaith supports wellness and health by maintaining a “Tobacco Free” campus environment. Our “Tobacco Free” environment has been established to minimize adverse health effects to everyone with whom we have contact. This policy applies to all tobacco products including cigarettes, cigars, pipes, herbal tobacco products and chewing tobacco. These items are prohibited from use on Baptist Health's campus or at any facility owned, leased or operated by Baptist (Excluding Paducah, as there are designated smoking areas). t understand that | will need to communicate with my treating physician or nurse to request nicotine replacement products, if needed. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further understand that if | leave my treatment area or any Baptist Health Facility, that | will nat be under nursing or medical supervision and may aggravate or worsen my condition and may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician. SMOKING CESSATION Wellness and Health Promotion: for smokers, those who are around smokers {second-hand smoke), or those who quit smoking and failed. Smoking cessation is quitting smoking. Smoking is a very difficult habit to break for most people. The chemical, nicotine, which you inhale inte your lungs from a cigarette, causes your brain to have more dopamine. Dopamine is a chemical! that makes you feel good. Nicotine has many more harmful! effects on your body. This is why it is so important to stop smoking. Nicotine is addictive, which means when your body is used to having nicotine, and then nicotine isn't there, you fee! strong physical cravings and a need to smoke. This is why stopping is so difficult. Why is smoking bad for me?

. Smoking increases your chance for having a heart attack, a stroke and/or cancer.

° Smoking decreases the amount of oxygen that gets to your brain, heart, and other organs.

. Smoking raises your blood pressure and makes it harder for your blood to circulate throughout your body,

. Smoking increases acid in your stomach, which leads to ulcers Why is quitting good for me? As soon as you quit smoking, your chances of having a heart attack, stroke, and other diseases stari to go down. The longer you do not smoke, the smaller your risk for these diseases and problems.

What is the hest way to quit?

¢ First, decide in your mind that you are going to quit. * Seta specific date to stop smoking and tell other people that you are going to stop smoking on your quit date. + Talk to your doctor about medications that can be used to help you quit. Over the counter and prescription are available. * Talk to ex-smokers about what helped them quit. * Join a support group for people who are quitting. Group Participation raises your chance of success by eight times. * Find friends or family members who are willing to help you quit or who will quit smoking with you. * Before your quit date, only allow yourself to smoke in one or two places. * Pay attention to when, where, and with whom you smoke. Notice what things make you feel like smoking. * Avoid the places, situations, and people that cause you to want a cigarette, at least at first when you are quitting. * Make a list of all of the good things that will happen if you quit smoking. * — Think of what you will do with the money that you will save by not buying cigarettes. * Begin an exercise program suggested by your doctor. ° Throw away all cigarettes, lighters, and matches before your quit date. | have rece Med the above [hformation about Baptist Health’s Tobacco Free Environment and smoking cessation. ACME * — The Active Cumulative Morphine Equivalent is calculated using a conversion formula from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control (CDC). The calculation is. based on the daily Morphine Equivatent Dose (MED) prescribed to the patient as of the last day In the date range requested for your report query (the 'To

Date’). The prescriptions used to calculate the ACME presented on this report are highlighted by bold text. The ACME is calculated based on prescription data reported to KASPER only and does not include prescription data from other states.

Daily MED * — The Morphine Equivalent Dose is a measure that equates different opioid potencies (based on route and dose) to a standard morphine equivalent. The Daily MED indicates the morphine equivalent value assigned to the daily dose for each Individual prescription record,

History of Present iliness:

1. preop Has uterine prolapse. Uterus pushed her tampon out last period and she is noticing it's getting a little bit lower. Having quite a bit of pressure as well. Periods are very heavy. She has had BTL so def. doesn't want any more children.

She also has significant leakage with stress. Keeps her bladder empty all of the time because she can't hold it with any stress. Does leak when bladder has anything in it. She has been doing kegels for some time and hasn't noticed any difference. Discussed options with pt. Will proceed with Total Vaginal Hysterectomy and TVT. R/B and complications discussed with pt. All questions answered.

2. MA Notes Ptis here for Hysterectomy and sling pre-op 11/5/18 w/ TG.

This 34 year old female presents for preop and MA Notes.

History of Present Illness:

1. preop Has uterine prolapse. Uterus pushed her tampon out last period and she is noticing it's getting a little bit lower. Having quite a bit of pressure as well. Periods are very heavy. She has had BTL so def. doesn't want any more children.

She also has significant leakage with stress. Keeps her bladder empty all of the time because she can't hold it with any stress. Does leak when bladder has anything in it. She has been doing kegels for some time and hasn't noticed any difference. Discussed options with pt. Will proceed with Total Vaginal Hysterectomy and TVT. R/B and complications discussed with pt. All questions answered.

2. MA Notes Pt is here for Hysterectomy and sling pre-op 11/5/18 w/ TG.

VAGINAL HYSTERECTOMY, TENSION FREE VAGINAL TAPING WITH OBTURATOR Procedure Note

Amy Mills 11/5/2018

Pre-op Diagnosis: Uterine prolapse Urinary stress incontinence

Post-op Diagnosis:

Uterine prolapse

Urinary stress incontinence Procedure(s): Total vaginal hysterectomy with left salpingectomy Retropubic tension-free vaginal tape Cystourethroscopy

Surgeon(s): Gilbert, Travis Daniel, DO

Anesthesia: Choice

Estimated Blood Loss: minimal

Specimens:

Order. vanes |. {Source -2|Comment

Procedure: The patient was taken to the operating room after informed written informed consent was obtained. She was prepped and draped in the usual sterile fashion in the dorsal lithotomy position after general anesthesia was obtained. We first placed in speculums anteriorly and posteriorly and grasped the cervix on each of the midline using a toothed tenaculum. We hydrodissected circumferentially around the cervix and then made a circumferential incision around the cervix. We then dissected anteriorly and posteriorly posteriorly we entered the cul-de-sac sharply and placed a retractor. We then dissected anteriorly to the point where we got into the anterior cul-de-sac. Next we took bites on each side using the Haney clamps, we then cut with scissors and suture ligated with 0 Vicryl. We held the sutures for manipulation later on. Next using the Generated on 6/11/19 9:59 AM Page 48




ea BAPTIST HEALTH” Baptist Health Corbin Mills, Amy oN 1 Trillium Way MRN: [PHONE-5524], DOB: [DOB-6556], Sex: F Corbin KY 40701-8727 Adm: 11/5/2018, D/C: 11/6/2018

Op Note - Encounter Notes (continued)

LigaSure device we took serial bites on each side of the uterus doubly cauterizing each pedicle and then cutting using the LigaSure. Once we got up to the fundus and the cornua we doubly cauterized it and delivered the uterus. We were able to easily remove the right fallopian tube at the left fallopian tube was little higher in the pelvis and we were on not able to reach it. We then attached the ipsilateral uterosacral ligaments to the vaginal cuff angles bilaterally and then closed the remainder of the vaginal cuff using interrupted 2-0 Vicryl stitches in a figure-of-eight fashion.

Next we performed the TVT. We isolated the mid urethra between 2 Allis clamps made a 1 cm incision and dissected out laterally and then retropubically. We then placed the TVT trocar through the incision and guided it retropubically and lateral to the urethra we hugged the pubic bone the entire time as we guided it suprapubically. We brought it out through the skin suprapubically. We did this bilaterally. Next we performed a cystourethroscopy making sure that we had not injured the bladder with the TVT trochars and we did not. We then pulled the mesh through and tensioned it loosely to where we could get a pair of Metzenbaum scissors under it easily. We irrigated it. We then closed this incision using a running locking stitch of 2-0 Vicryl.

All sponge lap and needle counts were correct there were no complications with the procedure. Findings: Uterine prolapse, normal bladder

Complications: none ‘Grafts or implants: TVT Mesh Travis Daniel Gilbert, DO

Date: 11/5/2018 Time: 9:03 AM

The following has been completed:

1. Identification of Patient, key family member(s) or patient surrogate

2. Identification of the responsible Practitioner (primary service)

5. Intraoperative anesthetic management and issue/concerns to include things such as airway, hemodynamics, narcotic, sedation level and paralytic management and intravenous fluids/blood producis and urine output during the procedure

6. Expectations/Plans for the early past-procedure period to include things such as anticipated course (anticipatory guidance}, complications, need for laboratory or ECG and medication administration

7. Opportunity for questions and acknowledgment of understanding of report from the receiving PACUACU team

) skin is nof uncommon. Non-serious reactions may include headache, fever, or a mild skin reaction, such as itching | orarasn. There 's no scientific evidence tnat directed donations are safer but | understand many patients fee! more comfortable Gesignating their own donors. Welcome to Baptist Health 9179 ADMIT DATE: il i il i TLL

Our mission Is to provide you with high quality individualized care that is safe and effective to meet your healthcare needs. We will provide you with education and instruction fo help with your pain managementrecovery, and we will strive for this fo be a successful process, This needs to be a team effort to include not only your care providers at the hospital, but you, the patient.

Our care goals are: ¢ To work together in providing your treatment in a respectful manner.

¢ To help you meet the goals of your treatment plan. ¢ To maintain a safe environment for the delivery of health care services at our hospital, « To protect the safety and well-being of all patients, visitors, staff and care providers.

We value you as a patient and want fo continue to provide you with high quality safe care and service. To do so, we need to outline the expectations that will foster an effective provider- patient relationship.

The following expectations are required to maintain an effective provider- patient relationship.

Patient Behavior Expectations

e | will comply with the medical treatment plan and recommendations developed between my provider and me.

« | will treat my care providers with dignity and respect, and | expect to be treated with dignity and respect.

¢ | understand that profanity, physical or verbal abuse, or any other form of violence against anyone will not be allowed or tolerated.

e | understand that unauthorized and/or illegal drugs are not allowed in the hospital.

* For my safety, | will not leave the nursing unit where | am a patient during my treatment unless accompanied by hospital personnel for diagnostic procedures or treatments.

« If leave the unit, it will be considered going against medical advice and | may be discharged from the hospital. For any future medical care, | would need to consult my primary care MD/Provider or go to an Emergency Room for further evaluation. Hospital staff have the right not to re-admit me for further care unless an emergency situation exists.

CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT: | voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of dlagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an off-site location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible Images during the surgical, medical, and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodefictency virus (HIV), if a provider orders testing for dlagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS

2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL; Physicians and certified registered nurse anesthetists rendering medica! treatment to patients fn the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such services are established by the providers.

3. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: | authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff to use and disclose information about.me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and disctosures include, but are not limited te, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their Intermediaries and review organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for benefits arising out of services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport to or from the Hospital; (b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home health agencles, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to which the patient may be transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospltal to obtain or maintain licensure or accreditation; (¢) to providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance reviews, and (f) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital's Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of medical records and billlng Information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric canditions, and/or communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. | also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical Device Tracking Act.

4, FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS: | agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other arrangements, | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for ail charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the reason, including but not timited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for payment under Title XVII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. | hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal ramedias on my behalf for collection agalnst any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medica! benefit plans, third party lfability carrler or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actlons necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any unpald account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or aitorney for collection. Topic: How to Quit (Not Started)

Point: Repetition (Not Started)

Description: It often takes multiple attempts to successfully quit “smoking. Community tobacco cessation programs increase the likelinood of success. Cognitive behavioral activities can be useful in learning to cope with smoking urges.

Withdrawal symptoms typically peak withln 1-2 weeks and may persist for months.

Lifestyle changes, such as avoiding activities assoclated with smoking, may be helpful.

Summary: Learning progress - not on file.

Point: Treatment Options (Not Started)

Description: Quitting | smoking may be easier with help. Options available to help quit include:

counseling

antidepressant medication

nicotine replacement therapy

coping and encouragement strategies

community support groups

Encourage discussion of opticns to determine what might work best. Summary: Learning progress - not on file.

Point: Managing Roadblocks (Not Started)

Description: Both exirinsic and intrinsic factors can trigger the desire to smoke. Extrinsic factors may include:

being around other smokers

drinking alcohol

stress

cost of medications to quit

lifestyle changes required

Intrinsic factors may include:

withdrawal symptoms, such as feeling ill, when trying to quit addictive nature of smoking

fear of failure

weight gain

tack of support or coping skills

depression

Summary: Learning progress - not on file.

Topic: Relevance of Quitting (Not Started)

Polnt: Tobacco/Smoking Risks (Not Started)

Description: Continuing to smoke exposes the smoker to both acute and tong-term risks. Acute risks include:

delayed wound healing

immune system Impairment

increased risk of respiratory infection shortness of breath

asthma or COPD exacerbation

infertility

sexual performance issues

Long-terms risks include:

heart disease

stroke

cancer

breathing problems

ulcers

gum disease

fingernail or teeth stains

inability to work or long-term disability Summary: Learning progress - not on file.

Point: Rewards of Quitting (Not Started}

Description: Even with long-term tobacco use, there are benefits to quitting today or as soon as possible. These benefits include: lower blood pressure

Point: VTE Prevention (Resolved)

Description: Following surgery, risk for devetoping VTE continues for up to 90 days.

To prevent VTE, early frequent ambulation is encouraged and should be continued at home. If confined to a bed or wheelchair, a caregiver will need to assist with arm and leg movement. Provider's instructions for continuing VTE prophylaxis medication should be followed at home. Learning Progress Summary
